If the value is < 0, then the absolute value specifies which boundary in b2.boundary.parameters is to be used. This will only work for the BCCON = 13 type boundary condition, for which a negative value for ionizing_core is required. Cannot be used in conjunction with 'eirene_ank_mods'. Use of this switch is not allowed if the core boundary condition is not of a flux type. Moreover, certain boundary conditions expressly include the flux of neutrals already (BCCON = 6, 19, 21, 23, 26, or 27), in which case use of this switch is redundant and should be avoided.

switch.name: eirene_background
switch.description: If eirene_background.eq.0, the ion velocities passed to Eirene to be used for the collisions are based on grad-B and ExB drifts (vadia + vaecrb). If eirene_background.eq.1, these velocities contain the full diamagnetic and ExB drifts (wadia + vaecrb). Note: recycling fluxes are always computed based on grad-B and ExB drifts only (and are not affected by this switch), because diamagnetic drift flows largely close within the sheath.

switch.name: eirene_sheath_pot
switch.description: If eirene_sheath_pot.eq.1, the sheath potential drop as computed by B2.5 (i.e. including effects of parallel currents, secondary electron emission, etc.) is passed to EIRENE to compute ion acceleration in the sheath. If eirene_sheath_pot.eq.0, the sheath potential drop is recomputed by EIRENE, usually assuming zero current and secondary electron emission.

switch.name: b2mndr_atomic_physics_rescale
switch.description: If atomic_physics_rescale is not 0, then the code will rescale the rates stored in b2frates according to the multipliers given in the b2.atomic_physics_rescale.parameters inputfile before making use of them.

switch.name: neoclassical_ic
switch.description: ..set the contribution ic in NEOART 0 --- classical particle flux 1 --- banana plateau contribution 2 --- Pfirsch-Schlueter contribution 3 --- both banana and PS 4 --- all contributions mind that B2.5 already calculates the classical transport ! avoid double transport, 0+4 for cross-checks only !

switch.name: b2mndr_solve_keps
switch.description: Main switch controlling the use of the k(-eps)-model. In this model, anomalous transport coefficients are determined self-consistently through the solution of an additional transport equation for the turbulent kinetic energy k (variable "kt" in the code) and possibly the turbulent enstrophy zeta (variable "zt" in the code). Note that both the k and k-eps models are currently under active development/testing, and should be used with care. In particular, the k-eps model (option '2') is not suitable for routine testing yet. If '0': k(-eps)-model not used. If '1': one additional transport equation solved for k. If '2': two additional transport equations solved for k and zeta. Warning: under development - expert use only! Note: solutions to these equations are only used to compute the transport coefficients if the switch b2tqna_transport_keps is set.

switch.name: b2tqna_transport_keps
switch.description: Compute transport coefficients based on k(-eps)-model. If '0': transport coefficients not computed based on k(-eps)-model. If '1': transport coefficients computed using k-model (i.e. based on kt). If '2': transport coefficients computed using k-eps model (i.e. based on kt and zt). Warning: under development - expert use only!

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_local
switch.description: If '0': use Larmor radius at OMP separatrix in k(-eps)-model. If '1': use local Larmor radius for each cell in k(-eps)-model.

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_iout
switch.description: If '1': print output for k(-eps)-model.

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_cd
switch.description: Multiplier for the diffusion coefficient in the k(-eps) model. Recommended range: 0.1 ... 1.0

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_shear
switch.description: Multiplier for the shear contribution to the diffusion coefficient in the k(-eps) model. Only affects results if ExB drifts are present.

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_heat
switch.description: Multiplier for the electron heat conductivity (w.r.t. diffusion coefficient).

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_heat_i
switch.description: Multiplier for the ion heat conductivity (w.r.t. diffusion coefficient).

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_visc
switch.description: Multiplier for the ion viscosity (w.r.t. diffusion coefficient).

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_sig
switch.description: Multiplier for the anomalous electrical conductivity (w.r.t. diffusion coefficient x qe x ne(omp)).

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_alf
switch.description: Multiplier for the anomalous thermo-electric coefficient (w.r.t. diffusion coefficient x sqrt(qe/te) x ne(omp)).

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_dkt
switch.description: Multiplier for the k-conductivity (w.r.t. diffusion coefficient).

switch.name: b2tqna_keps_dzt
switch.description: Multiplier for the diffusivity in z-equation (w.r.t. diffusion coefficient).

switchgroup.name: b2tfhi_fflokt*
switchgroup.description: fflokt and fconkt are fudge factors to approximate enhanced parallel transport of k due to term related to (fluctuations in parallel current) * (fluctuations in potential) In principle this models the same effect as skt_diss (thus: needs b2sikt_fac_sheath_* = 0.0).

switchgroup.name: b2tfhi_fflozt*
switchgroup.description: fflozt and fconzt are fudge factors to the transport of zt. See code for details.

switch.name: b2tfhe_vis_kt
switch.description: Multiplier to the perpendicular current due to Reynolds-stress in k-model.

switchgroup.name: b2tqna_keps_init*
switchgroup.description: Mechanism to enable a smooth transition between standard anomalous transport coefficients and k(-epsilon)-model coefficients. The final coefficients (dna0, vsa0, hcib, hce0, sig0, alf0) are linearly interpolated between the standard and the k(-epsilon) values based on keps_fac. keps_fac starts out as b2tqna_keps_init and the value is multiplied by b2tqna_keps_inc at each iteration.

switch.name: b2tfhi_fsigkt
switch.description: Multiplier for the parallel transport term of kt.

switch.name: b2sikt_model
switch.description: Model to compute the sources in the energy equations due to the k-eps model. Currently only a single model is available.

switch.name: b2sikt_style
switch.description: Currently only a single computation style for the sources in the energy equations due to the k-eps model is available.

switch.name: b2sikt_sheath_local
switch.description: If '0': use sound speed, connection length, and Larmor radius at OMP in k(-eps)-model. If '1': use local sound speed, connection length, and Larmor radius.

switch.name: b2sikt_kt_source_stab
switch.description: Numerical stabilization of sources. See code for details.

switch.name: b2sikt_fac_diss
switch.description: Multiplier for the dissipation term of kt in the SOL region.

switch.name: b2sikt_fac_diss_core
switch.description: Multiplier for the dissipation term of kt in the core region.

switch.name: b2sikt_fac_sheath
switch.description: Multiplier for the sheath loss term of kt in the SOL region.

switch.name: b2sikt_fac_sheath_core
switch.description: Multiplier for the sheath loss term of kt in the core region.

switch.name: b2sikt_fac_diss_core_mode
switch.description: If '0': use connection length in expression for dissipation term of kt. If '1': use b2sikt_fac_diss_lpar in expression for dissipation term of kt.

switch.name: b2sikt_min_source
switch.description: If '1': sets minimum source for k to zero.

switch.name: b2sikt_fac_aniso
switch.description: If '1': add anisothermal contribution to transport coefficients from k-(epsilon)-model.

switch.name: b2sikt_fac_vis_RS
switch.description: Multiplier for Reynolds-stress turbulent viscosity in k-model equations. Only active if ExB drifts are present.

switch.name: b2tfhi_fkt_hie
switch.description: Multiplier for transport of k associated with turbulent ExB heat fluxes.

switch.name: keps_anom_he_model
switch.description: If set to 0, uses multiplier 5/2 for the anomalous convective and conductive heat fluxes associated with anomalous density transport, as per the SOLPS5.2 physics model. If set to 1 (default), uses multiplier 3/2 for the anomalous convective and conductive heat fluxes associated with electrostatic ExB drift turbulence, consistent with the keps-model derivation. If set to 2 (experimental), additionally assumes the corresponding physical fluxes across surfaces/boundaries to have multiplier 3/2.

switch.name: b2mn_afn
switch.description: Main switch to turn on the AFN model for hydrogenic neutrals. If this switch is turned on, some default values are changed (see description of the corresponding switches).

switch.name: b2mn_spatial_hybrid
switch.description: Switch to turn on the spatially hybrid fluid-kinetic neutral model. For multispecies simulations, the hybrid approach can only be used for the hydrogenic neutrals. For other species, you can use either a fully kinetic or purely fluid approach.

switch.name: b2stbr_recycle_afn
switch.description: When set to 1, the AFN recycling boundary conditions are used for hydrogenic neutrals. When b2mn_afn.ne.0 and there is no specification of b2stbr_recycle_afn, the switch is automatically set to 1.

switch.name: b2stbr_afn_bcs_use_coarse
switch.description: When set to 1, the coarse representation of the integrated TRIM reflection coefficients is used for the AFN boundary conditions, which is expected to be sufficient. In case you want to use the original fine representation, use b2stbr_afn_bcs_use_coarse = 0. However, the fine representation is only present for D on Be, C and W.

switch.name: b2tqna_transport_afn
switch.description: When set to 1, the AFN transport coefficients are used for hydrogenic neutrals. When b2mn_afn.ne.0 and there is no specification of b2stbr_transport_afn, the switch is automatically set to 1.

switch.name: b2tqna_afn_vnn
switch.description: When set to 1, a collision frequency related to neutral-neutral collisions is added to the AFN transport coefficients. This is an ad-hoc model for fluid neutral transport in regions where plasma density is extremely low and CX effectively absent (e.g. below dome, far-SOL,...). When set to 0, the correction is not added.

switch.name: b2tqna_afn_vnn_ndiff
switch.description: When set to 1, perpendicular transport in the AFN model due to neutral-neutral collisions is modelled as density diffusion rather than pressure diffusion. When set to 0, it is modelled as pressure diffusion.

switch.name: b2mn_tn_style
switch.description: 0: solving the total energy equation for all ion + (fluid) neutral species together; 1: solving the total energy equation without the contributions from hydrogenic neutrals. It is still assumed that the hydrogenic neutrals have the same temperature as the ions, i.e. tn=ti. (not recommended to turn on); 2: separate energy equation for the hydrogenic neutrals (tn.ne.ti). For simulations with hydrogenic mixtures and b2mn_tn_style = 2, a total energy equation for all hydrogenic neutrals together will be solved assuming tn for each hydrogenic neutral species.

switch.name: use_auto_spatial_hyb
switch.description: Advanced hybrid option. Forbidden to use for multispecies simulations (ns > 1) and only in use for the strata with HYB_TYPE='A'. The local charge-exchange Knudsen number Kn is calculated at the boundary. If (Kn < auto_spatial_hyb_Kn_1) then the recycled atom is treated as a fluid. If (Kn > auto_spatial_hyb_Kn_2) then the recycled atom is treated kinetically. (otherwise) linear combination between fluid and kinetic.

switch.name: l_macro_afn
switch.description: Macroscopic length scale (in m) used to calculate the local Knudsen number.

switch.name: b2stbr_kn_b1
switch.description: Should be used in combination with MAXW in b2.neutrals.parameters (see description MAXW).

switch.name: b2stbr_kn_b2
switch.description: Should be used in combination with MAXW in b2.neutrals.parameters (see description MAXW).

switch.name: auto_spatial_hyb_Kn_1
switch.description: Should be used in combination with use_auto_spatial_hyb (see description use_auto_spatial_hyb).

switch.name: auto_spatial_hyb_Kn_2
switch.description: Should be used in combination with use_auto_spatial_hyb (see description use_auto_spatial_hyb).

switch.name: b2stbr_remove_fc_el
switch.description: When set to 1, the energy required for the Franck-Condon dissociation of the thermally released particles at the surfaces in the AFN boundary conditions is subtracted from the electron energy equation. It is not recommended to turn this switch on, because it seems to lower the electron temperature to unrealistic values.

switch.name: b2trcl_min_collisions
switch.description: Defines the style of the ion heat flux limit. If the number of collisions in the flux tube (determined by its average collisionnality) is larger than the value in the key, the old style of ion flux limit will be applied. Otherwise the new style (flux limit is defined by the number of collisions) will be applied.

switch.name: b2tlmv_cvsa_low_col_mltpl
switch.description: Additional multiplier for viscosity flux limit coefficient in area with low number of collisions. Applies only if b2trcl_min_collisions.gt.0.0

switch.name: b2stbc_delpo
switch.description: Potential drop used for BCENI/E = 15 when the potential equation is not solved (pot_eq.ne.1), in units of the local electron temperature.

switchgroup.name: b2tlc0_style_*
switchgroup.description: Determines the method for applying flux limiters to fluid neutrals. style.eq.0: old treatment where the radial and poloidal conductive fluxes are limited separately. style.eq.1: improved treatment (isotropic flux limiters) where the total conductive flux is limited, preventing artificial rotation of the flow.

switchgroup.name: b2tqna_keps_dna_min*
switchgroup.description: Minimum transport coefficients for use in k-epsilon model. It is also possible to use these minima for other transport models by setting b2tqna_limit_coeff.eq.1.

switch.name: b2tqna_limit_coeff
switch.description: b2tqna_limit_coeff.ne.0: the minimum transport coefficients dna_min, vsa_min, hci_min and hce_min are for whatever transport model is selected. b2tqna_limit_coeff.eq.0: the minimum transport coefficients dna_min, vsa_min, hci_min and hce_min are only used for the k-epsilon model.

switch.name: b2siav_cqip1
switch.description: Constant c_q1 in calculation of additional viscosity terms calculated in b2siav.

switch.name: b2sigp_phm0
switch.description: Multiplier for the pressure gradient term. Setting b2sigp_phm0.ne.1.0 should only be done for specific testing purposes.

switch.name: b2nxfv_phm0
switch.description: Multiplier for the convective term due to new form of ion viscosity term in the momentum correction equation. Applies to hydrogenic ions and fluid neutrals.

switch.name: b2nxfv_phm1
switch.description: Multiplier for the convective term due to new form of ion viscosity term in the momentum correction equation. Applies only to hydrogenic fluid neutrals.

switch.name: b2stbc_phm0
switch.description: Multiplier for radial diamagnetic current in BCPOT=12.

switch.name: b2stbc_phm1
switch.description: Multiplier for radial inertial current in BCPOT=12.

switch.name: b2stbm_internal_energy_sources
switch.description: internal_energy_sources.eq.0: the externally provided energy sources are total energy sources and a conversion to internal energy sources is performed. internal_energy_sources.eq.1: the externally provided energy sources are already internal energy sources.

switch.name: b2tfhe_anomalous
switch.description: Multiplier to the anomalous current.

switch.name: b2tanml_anomalous
switch.description: The correct switch is now b2tfhe_anomalous. b2tanml_anomalous is kept for backward compatibility. b2tanml_anomalous will only be read if b2tfhe_anomalous.eq.1.0.

switch.name: b2tqna_cfvma
switch.description: Anomalous velocity in parallel momentum balance equation.

switchgroup.name: b2tstbc_bc_ref*
switchgroup.description: Under-relaxation factors related to feedback schemes for boundary conditions.

switch.name: b2mndr_b2time
switch.description: Specifies the number of timesteps between writes of the b2time.nc time-dependent file. If b2time.gt.0, always writes out on the last timestep. If b2mndr_stim.lt.0, data from the current run is appended to the existing b2time.nc file, otherwise the file is overwritten.

switch.name: b2mndr_tally
switch.description: Specifies the number of timesteps between writes of tallies. If tally.gt.0, always writes out on the last timestep.

switch.name: b2mndt_moitlv
switch.description: Controls detailed monitoring output. moitlv.eq.-1 means no output, moitlv.eq.0 means output once per timestep, moitlv.eq.1 means output once per nstg0 iteration, moitlv.eq.2 means output once per nstg1 iteration and moitlv.eq.3 means output once per nstg2 iteration. See 'b2mndr_nstg?' description in Run section for more details.

switch.name: b2mndt_moqtlv
switch.description: Controls quick trace output. moqtlv.eq.-1 means no output, moqtlv.eq.0 means output once per timestep, moqtlv.eq.1 means output once per nstg0 iteration, moqtlv.eq.2 means output once per nstg1 iteration and moqtlv.eq.3 means output once per nstg2 iteration. See 'b2mndr_nstg?' description in Run section for more details.

switch.name: b2mndr_mvnum
switch.description: Specifies the maximum number of instances at which movie data will be output.

switch.name: b2mndr_mvinc
switch.description: Specifies the number of timesteps between movie output. If greater than 1 and the last timestep does not correspond to a planned frame, an extra endstate frame is added.

switch.name: b2mndr_plasnum
switch.description: Specifies the maximum number of instances at which extra writes of b2fplasmf.xxxx will occur.

switch.name: b2mndr_plasinc
switch.description: Specifies the number of timesteps between b2fplasmf.xxxx writes.

switch.name: b2mndr_cdfmovietim
switch.description: If non-zero, the movie file b2movies.nc is generated, containing several 2d quantities. Gives the real-time interval between writes of movie frames in b2movies.nc. If non-zero, must be at least as large as the simulation timestep.

switch.name: cdfmovie_fields
switch.description: Controls the quantities outputted to b2movies.nc. If cdfmovie_fields.ge.1 then only the main state variables (na, ne, Te, Ti, etc.) are written with each write to b2movies.nc. If cdfmovie_fields.ge.2 then additional quantities (e.g. fluxes, particle and energy sources) are also included.

switch.name: b2mndr_ntim_save
switch.description: Another option for plasma state file output. If greater than 0, gives the number of B2.5 full interations between successive write-ups of plasmastate files, in addition to the ones written at regular CPU intervals (see b2mndr_savecpu and b2mndr_plasmatim). Should be used, at the exclusion of other plasmastate write-up frequency settings, in conjunction with the Leuven Monte Carlo averaging scheme.

switch.name: b2mndt_av
switch.description: If b2mndt_av.gt.0, turns on computation of running averages.

switch.name: b2mndt_av_continue
switch.description: If b2mndt_av_continue.gt.0, continuously perform running averages.

switch.name: b2mndt_av_ntim_batch
switch.description: If ntim_batch.gt.0, number of iterations used to compute batch averages, written out in 'b2batch.nc'.

switch.name: b2mndt_av_ntim_run
switch.description: If ntim_run.gt.0, number of iterations used for writing running averages.

switch.name: b2mndt_av_batch_all
switch.description: If b2mndt_av_batch_all.gt.0, produces standard output for batch averages.

switch.name: b2mndr_plasmatim
switch.description: Another option for plasma state file output. If greater than 0, gives the real-time interval between successive write-ups of plasmastate files, in addition to the ones written at regular CPU intervals (see b2mndr_savecpu and b2mndr_ntim_save). Must then be at least as large as the simulation timestep.

switch.name: b2mndr_ids_save
switch.description: Frequency, in timesteps, at which an IDS is saved containing the plasma state. Can be used in conjunction with b2mndr_ids_time and b2mndr_ids_av. If used, an IDS time slice will be written at the end of run. If negative, a single IDS will be written at the end of the run. Will not work if running a "0 timesteps" simulation, in which case the use of b2_ual_write is recommended.

switch.name: b2mndr_ids_time
switch.description: Frequency, in simulation seconds, at which an IDS is saved containing the plasma state. Can be used in conjunction with b2mndr_ids_save and b2mndr_ids_av. If non-zero, must be at least as large as the simulation timestep. If used, an IDS time slice will be written at the end of run. Will not work if running a "0 timesteps" simulation, in which case the use of b2_ual_write is recommended.

switch.name: b2mndr_ids_av
switch.description: Frequency, in number of batch averages, at which an IDS is saved containing the averaged plasma state. Can be used in conjunction with b2mndr_ids_save and b2mndr_ids_time. The averaged plasma state is saved as a separate occurrence of the edge_profiles and edge_sources IDSs.

switch.name: b2wdat_iout
switch.description: If iout.eq.1, a large set of *.dat output files will be produced containing the values of a variety of code quantities, evaluated at the end of the B2.5 iteration. If iout.eq.4, an even larger set of output files will be produced, for the purposes of a full run analysis, evaluated in the individual routines where the quantities are used. For even more detailed debugging analysis, one should instead use "procedure_name"_iout.eq.1. The files and their content are fully described in the Output_description.pdf file in the $SOLPSTOP/doc directory and Appendix G of the SOLPS-ITER manual.

switch.name: get_residuals
switch.description: If get_residuals.eq.1, then the residuals of unsolved equations will be outputted into b2ftrace. Equations are unsolved if 'b2news_no_solve' is nonzero or any elements of the solveco, solvemo, solveee, solveei, or solvepo are set to .false. . By default, get_residuals.eq.0 and the residuals for unsolved equations are set to zero. The residual of the total momentum equation is nonzero when all momentum equations for parallel velocities are solved together. The residual of the total energy equation is nonzero when Te and Ti equations are solved together.

switch.name: b2wdat_append
switch.description: If append.ne.0, the output files produced by the b2wdat_iout.eq.4 switch are appended upon every write, instead of being rewritten every time.

switch.name: my_out_digits
switch.description: Specifies the number of significant digits with which the *.dat files are written out. Defaults to 6 in normal mode and 15 in debug mode. Must be positive.

switch.name: b2mndr_old_style
switch.description: If old_style.gt.0, old-fashioned (SOLPS4 style) output is added at the end of the b2mn.prt file.

switch.name: b2mndr_av_read
switch.description: If b2mndr_av_read.gt.0, the initial plasma is the averaged solution read from the b2faveri file. Check $SOLPSTOP/doc/SOLPS-ITER_Eirene_averaging.pdf for details.

switchgroup.name: b2mndr_*
switchgroup.description: The five switches above are safeguards numbers for when printing changes after a time step. The change is computed as: deltaX = abs((X(t)-X(t-1))/(X(t)+X_eps))

switch.name: b2mndr_trantim
switch.description: Produces a numbered 'tran' file every trantim real-time seconds. An endstate file is written if it falls between scheduled write-up times. Only available within the -DJET environment. If non-zero, must be at least as large as the simulation timestep.

switch.name: b2mwti_target_offset
switch.description: The diagnostic values from b2time.nc use guard cell values if target_offset.eq.0, and values from the neighbouring real cell if target_offset.eq.1. Fluxes are not affected.

switch.name: b2mwti_2dwrite
switch.description: Controls additional output to b2time.nc. If 2dwrite.ge.1 then a few 2d arrays (ne, Te, Ti) are written with each write to b2time.nc. If 2dwrite.ge.2 then fluxes, electric potential, kinetic energy, and fluid particle and energy source terms are also included (e.g., rsana, rsahi, rqrad).

switch.name: b2mwti_ismain0
switch.description: Index of the species used to create the 'dp3d?.last10' diagnostic files. Defaults to the neutral species associated with ismain. If no such species is declared, the default will be set to ismain. Is expected to either correspond to a neutral species or to ismain. Can also be used in b2ar.dat to specify the index of the main neutral CX species when resorting to the AMNS database (starting from version 1.3.2). If ismain is also defaulted, then will be 0.

switch.name: b2mwqt_style
switch.description: Specifies the amount of data that is written out to b2ftrace. See the manual (Section on b2yq) for full details.

switchgroup.name: b2stbr_*_netcdf
switchgroup.description: If tallies_netcdf.ne.0, the file 'b2tallies.nc' is created, which contains the regional tallies in CDF format. If b2mndr_stim.lt.0, data from the current run is appended to the existing b2tallies.nc file, otherwise the file is overwritten. If b2mndt_av_ntim_batch.gt.0, the file 'b2batch.nc' is created, which contains the batch averages of the tallies. If b2wall_netcdf.ne.0, the file 'b2wall.nc' is created, which contains the wall tallies in CDF format [written every b2wall_netcdf main calls]. If b2mndr_stim.lt.0, data from the current run is appended to the existing b2wall.nc file, otherwise the file is overwritten. If balance_netcdf.ne.0, the file 'balance.nc' is created, which contains all of the arrays required by the balance post-processing routines, in CDF format. If balance_average.ne.0, the balance arrays are averaged over the number of completed timesteps (itim).

switch.name: ank_tracing
switch.description: If ank_tracing.ge.1, additional tracing output from Andrei Kukushkin is produced, in files to be found in the tracing/ directory inside the run directory. The traces will be written every ank_tracing iteration. If b2mndr_stim.lt.0, data from the current run is appended to the existing files, otherwise the files are overwritten.

switch.name: b2stbc_diagno
switch.description: Controls level of output in b2stbc and subservient routines. Level 1 (diagno.ge.1) output includes wrong_flow warnings, transport coefficients at the (nx/2,ny) basis mesh position, the solution found for the given heat flux at constant temperature bc, and all feedback diagnostics. Level 2 output (diagno.ge.2) includes the integral current correction, the boundary sources on the first call, excessive sound speeds, and the iterative progress to the given heat flux at constant temperature bc. Level 3 output (diagno.ge.3) includes the boundary sources on each call, and the boundary.prt file listing all boundary cells.

switch.name: b2stbr_output
switch.description: Output flag for the first_flight model. If output.ge.1, the files start, end, and fort.88 are created, which contain information about the chords used in the model.

switch.name: b2npmo_vlct_diagno
switch.description: Output flag to control diagnostics related to use of ion_vlct_restrict switch. If vlct_diagno.eq.0, the velocity restrictions are applied silently. If vlct_diagno.eq.1 (default), the user only gets a count of how many times the velocity restriction has been applied for each species, if any. If vlct_diagno.eq.2, the user gets the full details of where and how large the applied velocity restriction was, if any.

switchgroup.name: eirene_savef3*
switchgroup.description: For coupled runs, the code will create two files named fort.30 and fort.31, containing the plasma geometry and plasma state, respectively, for use by Eirene. This happens irrespective of the setting of the two switches above. If however, the user wishes to obtain these files for a non-coupled run, then these two switches can be set to 1 and the files will then be produced at the end of the run. If savef31.lt.0, then a fort.31.###### file is produced after every B2.5 time step. Additionally, if savef31.ne.0, the fort.31 file will be updated before every Eirene call for coupled runs.

switch.name: b2mndr_inverse_ua
switch.description: If inverse_ua.eq.1, the code will produce a 'b2fstati_with-ua' file that contains the same plasma information, but with the opposite sign convention for the parallel velocity. The run will then proceed with the new velocities with their sign inverted.

switch.name: b2yrdr_ns
switch.description: New number of species desired when reading a new atomic rates file using b2yr.exe. Must be specified within b2yr.dat. To be used for checking atomic rates after bundling and before any plasma state files have been created or converted, i.e. when the number of species in the atomic rates file b2frates does not match the number of species declared in the run parameters file b2fpardf.

switch.name: b2srsm_diagno
switch.description: Controls level of output in b2srsm. If diagno.ge.2, output will be given on every call. If diagno.eq.1, output will be given on main calls only.

switch.name: b2ux5p_cpu
switch.description: If cpu.gt.0, prints out the time spent in the matrix solver.

switchgroup.name: b2ux*
switchgroup.description: Output flag for the matrix solvers. Larger numbers mean increasing output level.

switch.name: eirene_mc_output_style
switch.description: If nonzero, prints sources computed by Eirene. If greater than 1, prints them on every use of the recycling sources, not just when they are computed.

switch.name: ma28_nwrite
switch.description: If nwrite.gt.0, prints the content of the sparse matrix in the b2_matrix file, for the first nwrite calls.

switch.name: b2news_ncallout
switch.description: If the iteration number is equal to ncallout, then several output files 'b2ne_npmo', 'b2ne_xppb', 'b2ne_npco', 'b2ne+nppo' which detail the convergence behaviour and residuals.

switch.name: b2tqna_diagno
switch.description: If diagno.gt.0, outputs details of the calculations of neutral transport coefficients when using the new_df0 model. See switch b2tqna_new_df0 for more details.

switchgroup.name: b2mndr_idout.
switchgroup.description: idout0 and idout1 specify the desired selection of output segments; idout0 for output before the calculation and idout1 for output at the conclusion of the calculation. The generic description of either of these 'idout' variables follows. idout specifies the desired selection of output segments for printed and graphical output. Associated with each segment is a four-character word, which is identified on the printed output. (The same word may be associated with several segments.) A particular segment will be produced only if its associated word has a match, as defined by the routine strmas, in idout.

switch.name: eirene_format
switch.description: This switch is to be provided within b2yt.dat to indicate to the b2yt converter the format of the original Eirene input.dat file to be modified. The accepted values are (case-insensitive): 'old' for input files from SOLPS4.0 and SOLPS5.0 runs using 'old' Eirene_96 'new' for input files from SOLPS4.0 and SOLPS5.0 runs using 'new' Eirene_99 'facelift' for input files from SOLPS5.1 runs 'juelich' for input files from Juelich Eirene versions (2008 and younger) 'iter' for input files from SOLPS4.2/4.3 and SOLPS-ITER runs

switch.name: solps_version
switch.description: This switch is to be provided within b2yt.dat to indicate to the b2yt converter the format of the original fort.44 file to be modified. The accepted values are (case-insensitive): '4.3' for a fort.44 file produced from SOLPS4.3 runs '5.0' for a fort.44 file produced from SOLPS5.0 runs '5.1' for a fort.44 file produced from SOLPS5.1 runs '5.2' for a fort.44 file produced from SOLPS5.2 runs 'iter' for input files from SOLPS-ITER runs (no conversion necessary)

switch.name: ids_from_43
switch.description: This switch is to be used when running the b2_ual_write utility program to obtain an IDS from the SOLPS run results. If set to 1, the IDS will indicate that the results come from a converted SOLPS4.3 run with no additional calculation. By default, the IDS will indicate a SOLPS-ITER run.

switch.name: AFN_out
switch.description: If AFN_out.ne.0, boundary flux components of the advanced fluid neutral models are written out. See code for details.

switchgroup.name: b2news_potit*
switchgroup.description: Obsolete. Removed from code.

switch.name: b2news_potok
switch.description: Obsolete. Removed from code.

switch.name: b2news_ramp_slow
switch.description: If ramp_slow.eq.0 (default), the ExB and diamagnetic drifts multipliers are ramped on each call of b2news, otherwise they are only changed on the first call of the nstg loop on the timestep.

switch.name: b2mndr_use_9pt_stencil
switch.description: Master switch to turn on the 9-point stencil numerical treatment. If set to 0, reverts to the old 5-point stencil. Only usable with the SOLPS5.2 physics model. The perpendicular viscosity current terms are not yet available within this treatment.

switchgroup.name: b2mndr_min_ares*
switchgroup.description: Minimum residuals for stopping a run. All criteria must be met simultaneously for the run to stop. The continuity and momentum minimum residuals apply to the "ismain" species (See "Run" section).

switch.name: b2mndr_res_quit
switch.description: Threshold of the maximum residual among the equations solved below which the run is stopped. Only meaningful when compiled with FIXED_POINT and with adjoint AD code.

switchgroup.name: b2mndt_nstg_ares*
switchgroup.description: Minimum residuals for an internal solution loop to stop. Used in conjunction with (not in replacement of) the nstg(0:2) numbers. All nonzero criteria must be met simultaneously. The continuity and momentum minimum residuals apply to the 'ismain' species (See 'Run' section).

switchgroup.name: b2news_nsm*
switchgroup.description: Allows for solving only for the species range [nsmin:nsmax-1].

switchgroup.name: eirene_*
switchgroup.description: Upper and lower bounds used when writing out data for Eirene.

switch.name: eirene_M_max
switch.description: If the Mach number based on the total fluid velocity of a background species exceeds eirene_M_max, all velocity components passed to Eirene are restricted to make the Mach number equal to eirene_M_max. It is an additional restriction on top of eirene_ua_min and eirene_ua_max.

switch.name: eirene_print_minmax
switch.description: Print min and max values of te, ti, na & ua

switch.name: eirene_extrap
switch.description: If eirene_extrap.eq.1, then guard cell plasma parameter values are calculated from the neighbouring real cell values. If eirene_extrap.eq.0, the guard cell values are used unchanged.

switch.name: eirene_ank_mods
switch.description: If ank_mods.ne.0, then uses an additional scheme to ensure particle balance as the B2.5 solution evolves, due to the internal iteration scheme, away from the plasma background on which the Eirene sources were originally computed at the beginning of the time step. The user is referred to the text in $SOLPSTOP/doc/Source_Scaling_in_B2.pdf for a full description of the method used. Cannot be used in conjunction with 'eirene_ionising_core'. Should also not be used if Eirene is run in time-dependent mode.

switch.name: eirene_dpc_fix
switch.description: If dpc_fix.eq.1, then uses the true particle source from the stratum. If dpc_fix.eq.2, sets this particle source to zero. The equivalent of the old behaviour is dpc_fix.eq.0 and is wrong!

switch.name: b2stbr_eir_src_nhist
switch.description: If b2stbr_eir_src_nhist.gt.1, then Eirene sources are accumulated and a moving average is computed over nhist calls. Note that only one of the b2stbr_eir_src_nhist, eirene_neutr_avg, eirene_underrelax or eirene_averaging_multiple_calls averaging schemes can be used at a time.

switch.name: eirene_neutr_avg
switch.description: If eirene_neutr_avg.gt.0, then Eirene sources are accumulated and averaged until eirene_neutr_avg+1 steps, at which point the averaging process is reset. Note that only one of the b2stbr_eir_src_nhist, eirene_neutr_avg, eirene_underrelax or eirene_averaging_multiple_calls averaging schemes can be used at a time.

switch.name: eirene_underrelax
switch.description: If eirene_underrelax.gt.0, then an underrelaxation scheme is used for the Eirene sources, with an underrelaxation ratio of 1/eirene_underrelax. Note that only one of the b2stbr_eir_src_nhist, eirene_neutr_avg, eirene_underrelax or eirene_averaging_multiple_calls averaging schemes can be used at a time.

switch.name: eirene_underrelax_continue
switch.description: If eirene_underrelax_continue.eq.1, the underrelaxed Eirene sources from the end of the previous simulation are read in, to continue the run without restart effects. The underrelaxed sources are saved in b2favere. The procedure is as follows: Step 1: when activating eirene_underrelax for the first time in a simulation, eirene_underrelax_continue should be 0, as the saved sources are not yet present. Set b2mndt_av to 1 to write out b2favere at the end of a run. Step 2: When continuing the run, set eirene_underrelax_continue to 1. The b2favere from the previous run will be converted to b2faveri automatically by the b2run script. Note: the reading of the underrelaxed sources from b2faveri is independent of the reading of the averaged plasma states by '’b2mndr_av_read'.

switch.name: eirene_averaging_multiple_calls
switch.description: If eirene_averaging_multiple_calls.gt.1, then Eirene is called multiple times within a single time step, corresponding to the value of this switch, before a new plasma solution is computed by B2.5, and the Eirene sources are averaged over all performed calls before being used to compute the new plasma solution. Note that this is different from setting b2mndt_nstg1.gt.1, since in the latter case a new plasma solution is computed immediately after one call of Eirene, with this cycle being repeated b2mndt_nstg1 times. Also note that only one of b2stbr_eir_src_nhist, eirene_neutr_avg, eirene_underrelax or eirene_averaging_multiple_calls may be used.

switch.name: eirene_uub_style
switch.description: If eirene_uub_style.eq.0, compute poloidal velocities passed to Eirene from ua, vadia, wadia, vaecrb arrays. If eirene_uub_style.eq.1, compute poloidal velocities passed to Eirene from fna and fna_eir fluxes.

switch.name: b2news_area_fix
switch.description: Obsolete. Removed from code. New implementation supersedes treatment of area_fix=3.

switch.name: b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict
switch.description: If ion_vlct_restrict.ne.0, the parallel speed of ions and fluid neutrals is limited to be no more than M times the global plasma sound speed. In addition, if ion_vlct_restrict.eq.2, the ExB ion speeds are limited to be no more than M the local ion sound speed. M is given by b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict_M.

switch.name: b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict_M
switch.description: Determines M for ion_vlct_restrict.ne.0.

switch.name: b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict_core
switch.description: If ion_vlct_restrict_core.ne.0, the parallel speed of ions and fluid neutrals is limited to be no more than core_M times the global plasma sound speed, on closed flux surfaces. This option does not affect the ExB speeds. core_M is given by b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict_core_M. The diagnostics mechanism is also controlled by b2npmo_vlct_diagno.

switch.name: b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict_core_M
switch.description: Determines core_M for ion_vlct_restrict_core.ne.0.

switch.name: b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict_abs
switch.description: If ion_vlct_restrict_abs.ne.0, the parallel speed of ions and fluid neutrals is limited to max_ua, regardless of the Mach number. This option can be used to stabilize cases where the Mach restriction is not sufficient, but even more care is needed to make sure the limit is not too active in the converged result. max_ua is given by b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict_ua. The diagnostics mechanism is also controlled by b2npmo_vlct_diagno.

switch.name: b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict_ua
switch.description: Determines max_ua for ion_vlct_restrict_abs.ne.0.

switch.name: b2mndr_ua_max
switch.description: Puts a hard limit on the absolute value of the parallel velocity, but applies only to fluid neutrals and in case the spatially hybrid fluid-kinetic model is active (spatial_hybrid.ne.0). This switch was introduced when b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict only applied to ions. Now that b2npmo_ion_vlct_restrict also applies to fluid neutrals, the removal of b2mndr_ua_max can be considered. For now it is kept for backward compatibility with published results.

switch.name: b2nppo_restr_po
switch.description: If b2nppo_restr_po.ne.0.0, the electric potential is restricted such that its ratio (in absolute value) with the electron temperature (in eV) does not exceed b2nppo_restr_po. If b2nppo_restr_po is negative, the limit only applies to regions of negative electric potential. Only applies on open field lines.

switch.name: b2upht_rte_min
switch.description: If b2upht_rte_min.gt.0.0, the electron to ion temperature ratio is forced to not be lower than rte_min (i.e. Te/Ti >= rte_min).

switch.name: b2sihs_shivis
switch.description: If b2sihs_shivis.eq.1, then shivc and shiva (ion heat source due to viscosity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells. If b2sihs_shivis.eq.2, then shivc and shiva (ion heat source due to viscosity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells except the ones that belong to the core and target boundaries. If b2sihs_shivis.eq.3, then shivc and shiva (ion heat source due to viscosity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells belonging to the main chamber wall boundary.

switch.name: b2sihs_shi
switch.description: If b2sihs_shi.ne.0, then all b2sihs_shi* and b2sihs_shivis keys will be assigned to b2sihs_shi value as default (individual keys can still be overwritten by b2sihs_shi* and b2sihs_shivis that take proprity over b2sihs_shi).

switch.name: b2sihs_shidu
switch.description: If b2sihs_shidu.eq.1, then shidu (ion heat source due to divergence of velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells. If b2sihs_shidu.eq.2, then shidu (ion heat source due to divergence of velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells except the ones that belong to the core and target boundaries. If b2sihs_shidu.eq.3, then shidu (ion heat source due to divergence of velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells belonging to the main chamber wall boundary.

switch.name: b2sihs_shidd
switch.description: If b2sihs_shidd.eq.1, then shidd (ion heat source due to divergence of drift velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells. If b2sihs_shidd.eq.2, then shidd (ion heat source due to divergence of drift velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells except the ones that belong to the core and target boundaries. If b2sihs_shidd.eq.3, then shidd (ion heat source due to divergence of drift velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells belonging to the main chamber wall boundary.

switch.name: b2sihs_shedu
switch.description: If b2sihs_shedu.eq.0, then shedu (electron heat source due to divergence of velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells. If b2sihs_shedu.eq.2, then shedu (electron heat source due to divergence of velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells except the ones that belong to the core and target boundaries. If b2sihs_shedu.eq.3, then shedu (electron heat source due to divergence of velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells belonging to the main chamber wall boundary.

switch.name: b2sihs_shedd
switch.description: If b2sihs_shedd.eq.0, then shedd (electron heat source due to divergence of drift velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells. If b2sihs_shedd.eq.2, then shedd (electron heat source due to divergence of drift velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells except the ones that belong to the core and target boundaries. If b2sihs_shedd.eq.3, then shedd (electron heat source due to divergence of drift velocity) will be zeroed out in boundary-volume cells belonging to the main chamber wall boundary.

switch.name: b2tfhe_vis_per
switch.description: vis_per is a multiplier to the perpendicular viscosity current. If vis_per.ne.0.0, the electric potential equation is solved by the subroutine b2npp7 using a 7-point stencil. The value '1.0' is recommended for runs with drifts when perpendicular viscosity and corresponding perpendicular viscosity current is taken into account. Cannot be used in conjunction with the 9-point stencil numerical treatment. If used with the 5-point stencil treatment and with fluid neutrals, must be equal to b2tfnb_vis_per for numerical stability reasons.

switch.name: b2stbc_sheath_drift_fix
switch.description: If sheath_drift_fix.eq.0, then the drift velocity used in the sheath boundary conditions is the sum of diamagnetic and ExB contributions (old, but likely wrong, treatment). If sheath_drift_fix.eq.1, then the drift velocity used in the sheath boundary conditions is the ExB velocity only (recommended).

switch.name: b2stbc_BC2_cor9
switch.description: This switch concerns a 9-point correction for the Neumann type boundary conditions (BCCON/BCMOM/BCENE/BCENI/BCPOT/BCENK/BCENZ = 2) for non-orthogonal cells. If b2stbc_BC2_cor9.eq.0.0, gradients tangential to the boundary are ignored. The actual normal gradient then deviates from the one requested by the user. If b2stbc_BC2_cor9.eq.1.0, an additional term based on the vertex values of the boundary face is added to correct for the tangential gradient, to get the correct (up to interpolation error) normal gradient.

switch.name: b2stbc_fix_fch_in_fhe_sheath
b2stbr_potential_at_guard_cell
switch.description: If potential_at_guard_cell.eq.1, the electric potential used for the computation of the incident energy for sputtering yields is taken as the value in the guard cell. If potential_at_guard_cell.eq.0, the value from the neighbouring real cell is used instead.

switch.name: b2stbr_temperature_at_guard_cell
switch.description: If temperature_at_guard_cell.eq.1, the temperatures used for recycling and reflection are taken as the value in the guard cell. If temperature_at_guard_cell.eq.0, the temperatures are interpolated between the guard cell and the neighbouring real cell.

switch.name: b2trcl_conductive_limit
switch.description: When set to '1' (recommended default), the flux limit to the parallel electron and ion heat fluxes is applied through the associated transport coefficients. If 'b2trcl_conductive_limit' is '0', then the keys 'b2tfhe_lim_flux' and 'b2tfhi_lim_flux' are used to decide whether a flux limit is applied, to the fluxes directly.

switch.name: b2trcl_core_cond_limit
switch.description: If core_cond_limit.eq.0, then the heat flux limits due to chvemx and chvimx are not applied in the core. Switch only active if 'b2trcl_conductive_limit' .ne. 0.

switch.name: b2tlh0_flux_limit_style
switch.description: If '0', use the SOLPS5.0 scheme for neutral heat conductivity flux limits. If '1', SPb form to calculate parallel and perpendicular neutral heat conductivity flux limit (does not preserve symmetry, kept for backward compatibility reasons). If '2', modifies the SPb treatment for the flux limits to be applied on the transport coefficients directly. It is recommended '2'.

switch.name: b2tral_mode
switch.description: Switch to choose between various interpolation schemes for transport coefficients (Does not apply to pinch velocities vla or to the temperature-driven electric conductivity alf). mode.eq.-1: harmonic averaging mode.eq.0 : geometric averaging mode.eq.1 : arithmetic averaging (SOLPS5.0 formulation) mode.eq.2 : arithmetic averaging (SOLPS4.0 formulation)

switchgroup.name: b2tfh._*hybr*
switchgroup.description: Switches to toggle between the standard 5.0 hybrid scheme (no_hybr.eq.0) and the old 4.0 upwind scheme (no_hybr.eq.1). These apply separately to the electron heat, ion heat and particle conservation equations. If hybr2.ne.0, then a mixed scheme is used where only the conductivity is computed according to the 4.0 scheme. Obsolete. Not available for WG. Replaced by the 'discr_meth' switches.

switchgroup.name: ._upwind
switchgroup.description: If upwind.ne.0, then the SOLPS4 upwind scheme in b2tfhe_ and b2tfhi_ to obtain the heat fluxes. Obsolete. Not available for WG. Replaced by the 'discr_meth' switches.

switch.name: b2tfnb_pflux_cor
switch.description: If pflux_cor.eq.1 and fna_mdf is used, enforces that the integral particle flux across the domain boundaries computed from fna is equal to the same integral computed from the fna_mdf fluxes. Not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2trcl_cvsa_mltpl
switch.description: Artificial coefficient providing faster convergence to the neoclassical electric field but giving a distortion of the flows in the SOL as a side-effect. Can be applied <>1 during the convergence and turned off for the final stage of calculations. Use with caution.

switch.name: b2uxus_mult_nonzero
switch.description: Number of expected nonzero matrix elements per matrix row.

switchgroup.name: b2uxus_mult_solvdim*
switchgroup.description: Multipliers to the number of nonzero elements in the solution matrix for workspace arrays in the matrix solver.

switch.name: b2ux5p_style
switch.description: Choose the type of 5-point stencil matrix solver. Style.eq.0 = iluter, Style.eq.1 = slv5pt, Style.eq.2 = MA28. NOTE: Only style.eq.2 will give good results. Other values are NOT recommended! style.eq.0 and style.eq.1 are only applicable to linear geometries with no cuts and no isolated regions.

switch.name: b2ux7p_style
switch.description: Choose the type of 7-point stencil matrix solver. Style.eq.0 = iluter, Style.eq.1 = slv5pt, Style.eq.2 = MA28copy3. Style.eq.3 = SDRV from YSMP. NOTE: Only style.eq.2 will give good results. Applying style.eq.3 should be corrected and is no longer recommended. It might give slow convergence or even divergence of the potential equation. Other values are NOT recommended! style.eq.0 .or. style.eq.1 will return an error.

switch.name: b2ux9p_style
switch.description: Choose the type of 9-point stencil matrix solver. Style.eq.0 = iluter, Style.eq.1 = slv5pt, Style.eq.2 = MA28copy. NOTE: Only style.eq.2 will give good results. Other values are NOT recommended! style.eq.0 and style.eq.1 will return an error.

switch.name: b2ux5p_acpar
switch.description: Paremeter needed for iluter matrix solver.

switch.name: b2stbc_fchy_dia
switch.description: Multiplier to the neoclassical current and diamagnetic heat flux convective boundary conditions, also multiplied by facdrift. Adds a poloidal variation consistent with neoclassics and diamagnetic contributions to the heat flux boundary conditions. Should only be used with the 5.0 model and heat flux boundaries. Allows use b2stbc_integral_current if fchy_dia.eq.0, forbids it otherwise. Not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2stbc_fchy_dia_coreonly
switch.description: If fchy_dia_coreonly.eq.1, the neoclassical current and diamagnetic heat flux convective boundary conditions are only applied on the core boundary. If fchy_dia_coreonly.eq.0, those are applied to all boundaries, including PFR, North SOL edge and diverted island center boundary. Not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2stbc_neoclassical
switch.description: Real parameter which establishes boundary conditions for radial component of the current for North and South boundaries when these lie on closed flux surfaces. If b2stbc_neoclassical is 0 then the radial component of the current is zero. If b2stbc_neoclassical is not 0 the radial component of the current is given by the neoclassical value. b2stbc_neoclassical is superseded if facdrift.ne.0. This cannot be used in conjunction with b2stbc_integral_current below.

switch.name: b2stbc_cbc
switch.description: Multiplier to the ExB velocity for sheath boundary conditions BCMOM=13. Not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2stbc_integral_current
switch.description: If integral_current.gt.0, corrects current sources so that there be no surface current at the North and South edges of the edge plasma. The value of integral_current multiplies the correction term added to the current source. This setting is incompatible with the normal use of diamagnetic drift terms (facdrift.gt.0.0 .and. b2stbc_fchy_dia.ne.0.0) or neoclassical boundary conditions (b2stbc_neoclassical.gt.0.0). Not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2stbr_first_flight_dl
switch.description: Step length (in metres) for computing the first flight model chords.

switch.name: b2stbr_first_flight_no_of_flights
switch.description: Number of chords started from each start point in the first flight model.

switch.name: b2stbr_first_flight_table_size
switch.description: Workspace size given to the first flight table.

switchgroup.name: b2stbc_s*
switchgroup.description: Small level sources introduced in all cells.

switch.name: b2stbc_bcpot_16_step
switch.description: Frequency (in number of calls to b2stbc_phys) at which the constant value of the potential at the boundaries where BCPOT=16 is applied will be recomputed. Not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2stbc_stab_coeff_sheath_te
switch.description: Stabilizing term to the source associated with boundary condition BCENE=15, when bcene_15_style.eq.1 (default). To help suppress electron temperature oscillations, this parameter can be set to a positive value. The range 1-100 is recommended.

switch.name: b2stbc_stab_coeff_sheath_ti
switch.description: Stabilizing term to the source associated with boundary condition BCENI=15. To help suppress ion temperature oscillations, this parameter can be set to a positive value. The range 1-100 is recommended.

switch.name: b2npht_stab_shei
switch.description: Stabilizing term to the heat sources associated with ion-electron energy exchange.

switch.name: b2txcx_increase_transp_coefs
switch.description: Multiplication factor to the poloidal transport coefficients (electron and ion thermal conductivity, particle transport proportional to grad na and grad pa, viscosity). Must be greater than 1.0 to have an effect. Only acts on cells faces between two boundary cells. If b2txcx_increase_transp_coefs.gt.1.0, b2trno_set_chcb_0 will be assigned to 1.

switch.name: b2trno_set_chcb_0
switch.description: If set_chcb_0.eq.1, sets radial components of transport coefficients (electron and ion thermal conductivity, particle transport proportional to grad na and grad pa, viscosity) equals to zero on not field-aligned boundary faces (as defined by b2us_prep_Qalfmin). Also decreases the same radial components by a b2txcx_increase_transp_coefs factor on not field-aligned faces between two neighbouring boundary cells. Always on if b2txcx_increase_transp_coefs.gt.1.0.

switch.name: b2mndr_na_min
switch.description: Minimal density maintained in all cells for all species (in m^{-3}). It must be true that na_min is smaller than na_new, as well as any of the initial densities provided in b2ai.dat. This switch replaces 'b2mndr_na0eps' from SOLPS5.x. It is also used to avoid division by 0 when calculating kinetic neutral velocities from the kinetic flux densities in b2tinnt.F.

switch.name: b2mndr_na_new
switch.description: Initial density (in m^{-3}) put in all cells for all new species if not overwritten by initial state file. This switch replaces 'b2mndr_na0eps' from SOLPS5.x.

switch.name: b2mndr_use_min_na_numerics
switch.description: Use a species- and region-dependent minimum density (in m^{-3}). The values are imposed by 'min_na' in b2.numerics.parameters.

switch.name: b2upht_te_min
switch.description: Sets the minimum electron temperature value (in eV).

switch.name: b2upht_te_max
switch.description: Sets the maximum electron temperature value (in eV).

switch.name: b2upht_ti_min
switch.description: Sets the minimum ion temperature value (in eV).

switch.name: b2upht_ti_max
switch.description: Sets the maximum ion temperature value (in eV).

switch.name: b2upht_tn_min
switch.description: Sets the minimum fluid neutral temperature value (in eV).

switch.name: b2upht_tn_max
switch.description: Sets the maximum fluid neutral temperature value (in eV).

switch.name: b2upht_kt_min
switch.description: Sets the minimum turbulent ExB kinetic energy value (in eV).

switch.name: b2upht_kt_max
switch.description: Sets the maximum turbulent ExB kinetic energy value (in eV).

switch.name: b2upht_zt_min
switch.description: Sets the minimum turbulent ExB enstrophy value (in s^{-2}).

switch.name: b2upht_zt_max
switch.description: Sets the maximum turbulent ExB enstrophy value (in s^{-2}).

switch.name: b2news_guard_flows
switch.description: Prohibited switch. As of v3.2.0, there are no faces between guard cells anymore.

switchgroup.name: b2news_fac*
switchgroup.description: Parameters for spatial variation of the diamagnetic, ExB and viscosity drift term multipliers. These are allowed to have tanh profiles centered at tanh_a and of width tanh_b, where both quantities are in metres and measured along the outer midplane. The maximum value of facdrift and fac_ExB is controlled by their respective _start and _target switches, see Run section for details. NOTE: none of them have been converted to WG! For consistency with the naming convention of related variables: b2news_facExB_tanh_a is an alias for b2news_fac_ExB_tanh_a, b2news_facExB_tanh_b is an alias for b2news_fac_ExB_tanh_b, b2news_facvis_tanh_a is an alias for b2news_fac_vis_tanh_a, and b2news_facvis_tanh_b is an alias for b2news_fac_vis_tanh_b. Additionally, for all deep core cell rings up to and including the iy_nocoreExB-th, fac_ExB(ix,iy) is set to zero. This means that ExB contributions and flows will be zeroed out in those cells. For consistency with the boundary condition treatment, the value iy_nocoreExB.eq.1 is NOT recommended!

switch.name: b2stbc_istyle_cur_contr_on_S_and_N
switch.description: When set to '2', SPb form of adding currents on the South core boundary is included by using BCPOT=12, and on the South PFR and North boundaries by using BCPOT=13. It is recommended '2', in conjunction with the BCPOT=12 and BCPOT=13 settings, respectively. When set to '1', SPb form of adding currents on South and North boundaries is done explicitly in addition to other existing boundary conditions for the potential equation. The old 5.0 calculation is recovered by using the value '0'.

switch.name: b2stbc_istyle_fchi
switch.description: If '1', explicitly use the expression (bx*cs*na) of particle flux (fna) from boundary condition instead of fna, for boundary condition type BCPOT=11. Not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2news_no_b2sral_call
switch.description: If no_b2sral_call.eq.0, add an additional call to recompute the sources in b2news_ to reproduce the behaviour from SOLPS4.

switch.name: b2news_do_2nd_b2npco_call
switch.description: If do_2nd_b2npco_call.eq.1, perform a second call to the density equation solve to improve particle balance, as done in SOLPS4.

switch.name: b2news_re_eval_prtls_fluxes
switch.description: If re_eval_prtls_fluxes.eq.1, the particle fluxes are recomputed at the end of b2news_. This is necessary for rescaling of the Eirene sources during coupled runs, so this switch is superceded by use_eirene, and also needed to reproduce SOLPS4 runs.

switch.name: b2mndt_style
switch.description: If 0, refers to the obsolete SOLPS5.0 physics model, no longer supported. If 1, the SOLPS5.2 physics model is used, and b2news_ is called to compute the new plasma state. (default) If 2, the SOLPS5.2 physics model is used, and b2news_m is called instead to run the code in fully self-consistent time-dependent mode. B2news_m uses a different order of solving equations: first the density equations, then the parallel velocity, electrical potential and Te and Ti equations (and optionally also the Tn and k-epsilon equations, if requested).

switch.name: b2mndt_ckn
switch.description: If b2mndt_ckn=2, the 2nd order Crank-Nicolson time-stepping scheme is used, instead of the default 1st order implicit Euler scheme. This scheme can be used both in time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style=2) and in steady-state mode (b2mndt_style=1).

switch.name: b2mndt_dummy
switch.description: This switch is used internally in the code to initially run a dummy call to b2mndt, which is needed to start up the Crank-Nicolson time-stepping scheme (b2mndt_ckn=2). This switch is not meant to be changed by users! Please always keep it at its default value of 0.

switch.name: b2mndt_ckn
switch.description: If b2mndt_ckn=2, the Crank-Nicolson time-stepping scheme is activated. This is a 2nd order scheme, while the default 1st order implicit Euler scheme is 1st order. The scheme is meant to be used in time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style=2). It can in principle be used in steady-state mode (b2mndt_style=1) for false time-stepping, but whether this is beneficial or detrimental is still unclear.

switch.name: b2mndt_bdf
switch.description: If b2mndt_bdf=2, the Backward Differentiation 2 time-stepping scheme is activated. This is a 2nd order scheme, while the default 1st order implicit Euler scheme is 1st order. The scheme needs to be started up in the first time-step with a different scheme, which can be set with the switch b2mndt_startup. This scheme is meant to be used in time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style=2). It can in principle be used in steady-state mode (b2mndt_style=1) for false time-stepping, but whether this is beneficial or detrimental is still unclear.

switch.name: b2mndt_startup
switch.description: When using a backward differentiation scheme (b2mndt_bdf=2), this switch decides which other time-stepping scheme to use for start-up in the first time-step. If set to 1 (default), the implicit Euler scheme is used. If set to 2, the Crank-Nicolson scheme is used.

switch.name: b2mndt_var_style
switch.description: Option to use a cell-based spatially varying false timestep computed at runtime based on local criteria. Can only be used in steady-state mode (b2mndt_style=1). In their current implementation, the methods can be used in conjunction with region-based multipliers (DTCO etc.) but not with flux-tube-based appraoches (DTCO_FTS etc.) and not with the core suppression mechanism (CORE_DT_SUPPRESSION). If 0, no cell-based timestep multiplier. If 1, local timestep based on the electron temperature. The baseline timestep (b2mndr_dtim) is applied below b2mndt_var_te_min. Above b2mndt_var_te_max, the baseline timestep is multiplied by b2mndt_var_mult. Log-log interpolation is used in between. If 2, local timestep based on the ratio of the source strengths compared to the particle/momentum/energy content of each cell. dt2 = abs(content)/abs(S)/Fs, where 'content' is the particle, parallel momentum, ion heat, or electron heat content of a cell, 'Fs' is a safety factor, and 'S' is the strength of the source terms. The included contributions are those computed by EIRENE, and B2.5 contributions due to ionization, recombination and CX (e.g. ionization between higher ionization stages or CX when using fluid neutrals). Other source contributions are not included because they are only computed after the calculation of the timestepping sources. The final timestep is determined as dt = min(max(dt2,b2mndr_dt),b2mndr_dt*b2mndt_var_mult), such that the user can retain control over the minimum and maximum timestep. If 3, CFL-criterion based on drift velocities. Highly experimental. Still under development. See code for details. If 4, combination of 3 and 2. Highly experimental. Still under development. See code for details.

switch.name: b2mndt_var_mult
switch.description: Maximum multiplier to the timestep when using b2mndt_var_style > 0.

switch.name: b2mndt_var_te_min
switch.description: The electron temperature below which the baseline timestep is used when using b2mndt_var_style = 1.

switch.name: b2mndt_var_te_max
switch.description: The electron temperature above which the maximum timestep is used when using b2mndt_var_style = 1.

switch.name: b2mndt_var_style2_SF
switch.description: Safety factor Fs when using b2mndt_var_style = 2 or 4.

switch.name: b2mndt_var_style3_Cmax
switch.description: CFL (Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy) limit when using b2mndt_var_style = 3 or 4.

switch.name: b2mndt_var_style_nfilter
switch.description: When using b2mndt_var_style = 3, carry over the minimum timestep between neighbours nfilter times. (Highly experimental).

switch.name: b2mndt_ntim_step_out
switch.description: When set to a value of 'K', residuals at each K-th step will be written in b2ftrace. It helps to avoid a huge b2ftrace files during long time run.

switch.name: b2mndt_calc_err_step_out
switch.description: When set to a value of 'K', the subroutine calc_err will be run at each K-th step. It allows to output the error at desired times during a time-dependent MMS test.

switchgroup.name: b2stb*
switchgroup.description: Feedback relaxation parameters. See Physics section for individual feedback quantities.

switch.name: b2stbc_cbsnafac
switch.note: NOT YET CONVERTED IN WG CODE

switch.name: b2stbc_nesepm_gamma
switch.note: NOT YET CONVERTED IN WG CODE

switchgroup.name: b2stbc_type13_*
switchgroup.description: Additional feedback strength adjustment parameters for BCCON=13 boundary condition, density adjusted to match a specific flux. The density is adjusted by a factor of: (1.0_R8+CONPAR(IS,IB,2)*(DFS-FS)/ (abs(DFS)+abs(FS)+abs(type13_norm)+abs(NAS*type13_fac))) where FS is the current particle flux, DFS is the desired particle flux, and NAS the current volume-averaged density. The desired flux DFS is computed as CONPAR(IS,IB,1)+CONPAR(IS,IB,3) where the first number is provided directly by the user in b2.boundary.parameters and the second is internally set to correspond to the re-entry of ionised neutrals that have crossed into the core for cases run with Eirene where eirene_ionising_core is activated. See also the description of 'eirene_ionising_core'. Not yet available for WG.

switchgroup.name: heatdiff1D_*
switchgroup.description: Parameters related to solving the temperature equation for the target plate elements in depth. If linlog.eq.1, a linear subdividing of the plate element is used. If linlog.eq.2, a logarithmic subdividing is used, with the surface layer being ratio times thinner than the last layer in the bulk. ratio must be larger than 1.

switch.name: b2trno_flux_limit_to_dpa
switch.description: If '1', flux limit to neutrals contribution to dpa0 - the diffusion coefficient is applied in b2tlc0.F. It is recommended '1'. b2tlc0.F has the flux limit parameters α and γ which are given by 'b2tlc0_alpha' and 'b2tlc0_gamma'. 'b2tfnb_alpha' and 'b2tlc0_alpha' cannot be different from zero simultaneously. 'b2tfnb_alpha' gives another form of flux limit which is applied to the whole particle flux.

switch.name: b2trno_flux_limit_to_vsa
switch.description: If '1', flux limit to neutrals contribution to vsa0 - the viscosity is applied in b2tlv0.F. It is recommended '1'. b2tlv0.F has the flux limit parameters α and γ which are given by 'b2tlv0_alpha' and 'b2tlv0_gamma'.

switchgroup.name: b2mndr_*fb*
switchgroup.description: Experimental: allows for density feedback of the number of electrons accompanying species 'isfb' at location (ixfb,iyfb). Note that ixfb and iyfb are real numbers!

switch.name: b2mndt_use_b2srst
switch.description: Switches off the stabilization of the source coefficients.

switch.name: b2mndt_rxf
switch.description: Main under-relaxation parameter. The default value is 0.5 in steady-state mode (b2mndt_style.eq.1). Setting this switch to zero prevents updating of the principal code quantities. In time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style.eq.2), the default value is 1.0. It can be set lower than 1, provided that the following relationship is satisfied: DTxx=1/(1-(1-rxf)^nstgJ). DTxx are the time-step multipliers (xx=CO,MO,EE,EI,...) and nstgJ the inner iterations (J=0,1,2). For example, if rxf=0.5 and nstgJ=1, then it must be DTxx=2. Tip: If nstgJ is high enough, DTxx tends to 1 for any rxf, and no time-step multipliers need to be changed.

switchgroup.name: b2news_xfm.
switchgroup.description: Under-relaxation parameters for the parallel momentum, continuity, potential and energy equations respectively. Setting these switches to zero prevents updating of the associated code quantities.

switchgroup.name: b2npco_pcm.
switchgroup.description: Specifiy multipliers to Rhie-Chow corrections from particle equations Not used (and default set to zero) if running in time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style.eq.2). b2npco_pcm0 is a multiplier for the update on the parallel velocity following the pressure-correction equation. b2npco_pcm1 is a multiplier for the Rhie-Chow terms in the particle fluxes.

switch.name: b2npco_rxg
switch.description: rxg specifies a special under-relaxation parameter. A term of the form "abs(residual)/rxg" is added to the diagonal of the matrix of the correction equation, with the effect of limiting the computed correction. rxg is dimensionless and of order unity; smaller values of rxg imply stronger damping. Not used if running in time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style.eq.2).

switchgroup.name: b2npht_pcm*
switchgroup.description: Specifies multipliers to Rhie-Chow corrections from heat equations. Not used (and default set to zero) if running in time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style.eq.2). b2npht_pcm0 is a multiplier for the update on the parallel velocity following the update of Ti and Te. b2npht_pcm1 is a multiplier for the Rhie-Chow terms in the flow fields of ion, electron, and neutral heat.

switch.name: b2npht_rxg
switch.description: rxg specifies a special under-relaxation parameter. A term of the form "abs(residual)/rxg" is added to the diagonal of the matrix of the correction equation, with the effect of limiting the computed correction. rxg is dimensionless and of order unity; smaller values of rxg imply stronger damping. Not used if running in time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style.eq.2).

switch.name: b2npht_style
switch.description: When set to '1', SPb form of the program b2sihs_ is called. b2npht_style.eq.0 no longer available for WG.

switch.name: b2npmo_rxg
switch.description: Normalisation factor for the parallel momentum equation. Not used when running in time_dependent mode (b2mndt_style.eq.2).

switch.name: b2news_poteq
switch.description: If poteq.eq.0, the potential equation is jumped over and not solved. If poteq.eq.2, the potential is set to 3.1*Te/qe as per SOLPS4.0. If poteq.eq.1, the potential equation is solved according to the no_solve switch settings. If poteq.ne.1, then 'b2tfhe_no_current' must be set to '1'.

switch.name: b2nxdv_style
switch.description: When set to '1', the total friction force cancel is not calculated at the guard boundary cells. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2nxfc_style
switch.description: style specifies the precise form of interpolation used in the computation of flcb and cvcb. When set to '1', SPb form of the transport terms in the momentum correction equation is used. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2nxfx_style
switch.description: When set to '1', SPb form of an expression that occurs in the electron-atom thermal force is used. Only used if b2sigp_style.ne.2. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2sifr_styl0
switch.description: Specify the type of linearisation used in the thermal force term.

switch.name: b2tfhe_mode_ehx
switch.description: Switch to choose between various discretization schemes when computing the poloidal electric field. See code for details.

switch.name: b2tfhe_mode_ehy
switch.description: Switch to choose between various discretization schemes when computing the radial electric field. See code for details.

switch.name: b2sigp_style
switch.description: When set to '1', SPb form of the pressure gradient term on the right hand of the momentum balance equation is used. When set to '2', the parallel current term contains a correction due to impurities, when Z_eff is not equal to the average plasma ion charge. Only usable if the potential equation is solved simultaneously (b2news_poteq.eq.1). The default value of '2' is recommended for cases with one main hydrogenic species (or at least where the main species is the lightest one) and any amount of impurities. It is not guaranteed to yield correct results for hydrogen isotopic mixtures or non-hydrogenic plasmas.

switch.name: b2sigp_pressure_restriction
switch.description: When set to '1', a CFL-like restriction is applied to the pressure gradient term for minority ions and for neutrals species.

switch.name: b2xzdd_zero_dead_and_core
switch.description: If 1 then zero passed sources in dead regions, If 2 zero passed sources in dead regions and core boundary cells If 0 then SKIP

switchgroup.name: b2sihs_rf.
switchgroup.description: rf0-4 are numerical parameters, see the code. All must be non-negative and of order unity; larger values are meant to provide more stabilisation. Some or all may be set to zero and removed eventually. I have had some experience to indicate that rf0, rf2, rf4 may have a useful function; however, later changes in the code may have made all these parameters superfluous.

switchgroup.name: b2srdt_phm.
switchgroup.description: Multipliers to the density, parallel momentum, heat, potential, and electron particle time-derivative source terms, respectively. 'phm4' is normally zero since the potential equation contains no source terms.

switchgroup.name: b2srst_rf*
switchgroup.description: (rf0/1/2/3 for numerical stabilisation)

switch.name: b2stcx_rg0
switch.description: (rg0 for numerical stabilisation; needs experiments.)

switch.name: b2stcx_styl0
switch.description: Specifies the type of linearisation used in the charge exchange momentum source term.

switch.name: eirene_mc_linearisation
switch.description: Specifies the type of linearisation used in the sources derived from the Monte Carlo neutrals. If linearisation.eq.0, all sources are fed to the constant term, if linearisation.eq.1, positive terms go in the constant term, and negative terms in the proportional term. 'eirene_mc_linearization' is an alias for this switch.

switch.name: b2stbm_linearisation
switch.description: Same as above, but applied to the sources coming for an externally coupled code providing plasma sources, for instance a kinetic treatment of trace impurity ions. 'b2stbm_linearization' is an alias for this switch.

switch.name: b2stbm_impgyro_mod
switch.description: Specifies the frequency (in units of full B2.5 timesteps) at which an externally coupled code providing additional source (for instance a kinetic treatment of trace impurity ions such as IMPGYRO) should be called.

switchgroup.name: b2stel_r*
switchgroup.description: Damping coefficient applied to the atomic physics sources.

switch.name: b2stel_styl0
switch.description: Specifies the type of linearisation used in the charge exchange momentum source term.

switch.name: b2tfcc_xfac
switch.description: Multiplier to the pressure force term.

switch.name: b2tfhe_mdf
switch.description: If '1', SPb new form of calculating electron heat flux is used. It is recommended '1' for runs with drifts.

switch.name: b2tfhe_no_current
switch.description: If no_current.eq.1, all currents are set to zero. The setting no_current.eq.1 must be used if the potential equation is not explicitly solved for, i.e. for b2news_poteq.ne.1.

switch.name: b2tfhe_prl_cur
switch.description: Multiplier to the parallel current.

switch.name: b2tfhi_mdf
switch.description: If '1', SPb new form of calculating ion heat flux is used. It is recommended '1' for runs with drifts.

switch.name: b2tfnb_drift_style
switch.description: When set to '0', drift velocities are calculated in cell centers (NO LONGER SUPPORTED IN WG CODE). When set to '1', drift velocities are calculated in cell faces. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2tfnb_poleldr
switch.description: Multiplier to the poloidal electrical drift velocity.

switch.name: b2tfnb_radeldr
switch.description: Multiplier to the radial electrical drift velocity.

switch.name: b2tfnb_poldidr
switch.description: Multiplier to the poloidal diamagnetic drift velocity.

switch.name: b2tfnb_raddidr
switch.description: Multiplier to the radial diamagnetic drift velocity.

switch.name: b2tfnb_mdf
switch.description: If '1', SPb new form of calculating particle flux is used. It is recommended '1' for runs with drifts.

switch.name: b2upht_stylec
switch.description: (stylec is a numerical switch, needs experiments)

switch.name: b2usmo_cfc0
switch.description: Linearisation constant.

switch.name: b2srsm_enable
switch.description: If enable.ne.0, then the sources for particle, parallel momentum, electron and ion heat are rescaled, for non-boundary cells, if the local timestep exceeds the physics timescale implied by those sources. enable.ne.0 not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2tinnt_style
switch.description: Integer switch to choose the numerical treatment of the ion pressure gradient calculation for the ion-neutral current. If b2tinnt_style.eq.0, then the chain rule is first applied to the product of density and temperature, and (grad n)/n is rewritten as grad(log(n)). If b2tinnt_style.eq.1 (default), then the gradient of the product of density and temperature is calculated directly.

switch.name: b2stbr_phys_lin_shi0
switch.description: Determines the linearisation of the boundary condition heat source due to neutral recycling/reflection when erecyc in used. Not used for advanced fluid neutral (AFN) model. lin_shi0.eq.0: put source in shi0(iCv,0). lin_shi0.eq.1: put source/ti in shi0(iCv,1).

switchgroup.name: b2npmo_discr_meth*
switchgroup.description: Determines the discretization method for the calculation of the fluxes. discr_meth.eq.0: central scheme discr_meth.eq.1: upwind scheme discr_meth.eq.2: SOLPS5 hybrid scheme discr_meth.eq.3: SOLPS4 continuity scheme. Not yet available for WG.

switch.name: b2tfnb_style_int_vel
switch.description: Determines the interpolation weights to get the parallel velocities at the cell faces. style_int_vel.eq.0: interpolation based on cell volumes. style_int_vel.eq.1: interpolation based on cell connector lengths. Only the combination b2tfnb_style_int_vel=0 and b2sihs_style_int_vel=0 works robustly for now. Other combinations gave rise to oscillations. More research needed.

switch.name: b2sihs_style_int_vel
switch.description: Determines the interpolation weights to get the parallel velocities at the cell faces for the calculation of divergence ua. style_int_vel.eq.0: interpolation based on cell connector lengths. style_int_vel.eq.1: interpolation based on cell volumes. Only the combination b2tfnb_style_int_vel=0 and b2sihs_style_int_vel=0 works robustly for now. Other combinations gave rise to oscillations. More research needed.

switch.name: b2ardr_fix_cx
switch.description: It is used to 'correct' the CX data 0 => do not fix 1 => only fix H if CX data is < 1e-40 [default] 2 => fix if CX data is < 1e-40 3 => fix H 4 => fix all At the moment the only species with CX data in ADAS is C. Be careful with options that use the fit formula other than for H. See the comments in ratstr.F for the origin of the fit formula used to 'fix' the CX data.

switch.name: b2ardr_no_weisheit
switch.description: When set to 1, disables the correction of the hydrogen atomic rate data with the Weisheit data using ratwei. Must be set to 1 when using b2ardr_amjhydhel. *** Use with caution! ***

switch.name: b2ardr_amjhydhel
switch.description: When not set to 0, reads reaction cards from the AMJUEL/HYDHEL database files to overwrite rates for neutral hydrogenic species. The list of reaction cards to be used should be given in the b2ar.dat file. Cannot be used in conjunction with no_weisheit.eq.0.
