switch.description: When the B2.5 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(SOURCES_TIME_MOD), reaches or exceeds SOURCES_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from SOURCES_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist if the ELM count (here time/SOURCES_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if SOURCES_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: SOURCES_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: If SOURCES_TIME_MOD is greater than 0, time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle at which a new PROFILE namelist is read. Otherwise, B2.5 simulation time, in seconds, when to read the next PROFILE namelist file from SOURCES_FILENAME. Only active if SOURCES_TIME_SWITCH is greater than 0.

introduction.description: Allows the user to set transport coefficients profiles. Only used is 'b2tqna_inputfile' is set to 1. Overwrites b2ah.dat, b2mn.dat and b2.transport.parameters. See SOLPS manual for details.

switch.name: NDATA
switch.description: Number of points over which the source profile of (kind_data,kind_coef,is) is defined. Should not exceed NY+2. If KIND_DATA=1, the data is expressed as a profiles in physical distance from the separatrix (in metres). If KIND_DATA=2, the data is expressed as a profile in flux distance from the separatrix (not yet available).

switch.name: TDATA
switch.description: For (i,ir,ik,is) in (1:3,1:NY+2,1:NKIND_COEFF,0:NS), TDATA(1,ir,:,:) contains the radial location of the profile point (ir). By default, these are measured at the outer midplane, as distance to the separatrix in metres. The user can change this by setting the 'set_transport_i[xy]ref' switches to choose a different location for the distance reference. TDATA(2,ir,:,:) contains the transport profile value at point (ir). TDATA(3,ir,:,:) contains the ELM transport profile value at point (ir). KIND_COEFF=1 means density-driven particle diffusivity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=2 means pressure-driven particle diffusivity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=3 means ion heat diffusivity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=4 means electron heat diffusivity KIND_COEFF=5 means poloidal pinch velocity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=6 means radial pinch velocity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=7 means viscosity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=8 means field-driven radial current conductivity KIND_COEFF=9 means temperature-driven radial current conductivity

switch.name: ADDSPEC
switch.description: If ADDSPEC(is,ikind,spec).ge.0, then the transport coefficient profile of type (ikind) from species (spec) is also used for species with index ADDSPEC(is,ikind,spec).

switch.name: TRANSPORT_IP_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /TRANSPORT/ namelist.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_IP_TIME_MOD
switch.description: When the B2.5 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(TRANSPORT_IP_TIME_MOD), reaches or exceeds TRANSPORT_IP_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from TRANSPORT_IP_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist if the ELM count (here time/TRANSPORT_IP_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if TRANSPORT_IP_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_IP_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: If TRANSPORT_IP_TIME_MOD is greater than 0, time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle at which a new TRANSPORT namelist is read. Otherwise, B2.5 simulation time, in seconds, when to read the next TRANSPORT namelist file from TRANSPORT_IP_FILENAME. Only active if TRANSPORT_IP_TIME_SWITCH is greater than 0.

switch.name: REGION_FLAGS
switch.description: If region_flags(ireg,ikind) is .true. (default), then the transport parameters profiles for region (ireg) and kind (ikind) are used.

switch.name: NO_PFLUX
switch.description: If .true., transport coefficients profiles are not implemented in the private flux regions.

switch.name: NO_DIV
switch.description: If .true., transport coefficients profiles are not implemented in the divertor regions.

switch.name: POLOIDAL_SCALING
switch.description: If .true., then the transport coefficients profiles from the current b2.transport.inputfile are increased by a factor of 1.0+Gaussian where Gaussian is a Gaussian profile in the poloidal direction of amplitude SCALING_STRENGTH extending from SCALING_IX_BEGIN to SCALING_IX_END inclusively. The profile has a decay length of SCALING_WIDTH (in units of the number of poloidal cells).

switch.name: SCALING_STRENGTH
switch.description: See above.

switch.name: SCALING_WIDTH
switch.description: See above. Defaults to about 1/3 of the interval over which the scaling is to be done.

switch.name: SCALING_IX_BEGIN
switch.description: See above.

switch.name: SCALING_IX_END
switch.description: See above.

switch.name: ELM_TIME_BEGIN
switch.description: Time (in seconds) modulo ELM_TIME_PERIOD at which the ELM phase begins and the ELM data must be used.

switch.name: ELM_TIME_END
switch.description: Time (in seconds) modulo ELM_TIME_PERIOD at which the ELM phase ends and the ELM data is no longer used.

switch.name: ELM_TIME_PERIOD
switch.description: Indicates the real frequency of simulated ELMs. See above for usage. If zero, no ELM profiles are used.

switch.name: ELM_IX_BEGIN
switch.description: Poloidal position at which the ELM profile starts to be applied.

switch.name: ELM_IX_END
switch.description: Poloidal position at which the ELM profile ends being applied.

introduction.description: Read if file is present. Used internally for regulation and rescaling of volume recombination sources and restarts. Superceded by use of 'eirene_dpc_fix' switch. See code in eirene_mc and description in b2cdci for details.

switch.name: SAVED_VOLREC
switch.description: Contains the last value of the strength of volume recombination sources from stratum (istra).

introduction.description: Used to specifiy time multipliers per equation and per region for the B2.5 solver. Active if 'b2srdt_numerics_namelist' is set to 1 or greater. It is recommended to use the same multiplier for all equations within one region. The time multipliers should not be used if running in time-dependent mode (b2mndt_style.eq.2).

switch.name: DTCO
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the continuity equation of species (is) in region (ireg).

switch.name: DTMO
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the parallel momentum equation of species (is) in region (ireg).

switch.name: DTEE
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the electron heat equation in region (ireg).

switch.name: DTEI
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the ion heat equation in region (ireg).

switch.name: DTEN
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the fluid neutral heat equation in region (ireg).

switch.name: DTFTS
switch.description: List of flux tube indices for which individual timestep multipliers can be applied.

switch.name: DTCO_FT
switch.description: Individual timestep multiplier to all continuity equations applied in the corresponding flux tube from the DTFTS list. Must be positive.

switch.name: DTMO_FT
switch.description: Individual timestep multiplier to all parallel momentum equations applied in the corresponding flux tube from the DTFTS list. Must be positive.

switch.name: DTEE_FT
switch.description: Individual timestep multiplier to the electron heat equation applied in the corresponding flux tube from the DTFTS list. Must be positive.

switch.name: DTEI_FT
switch.description: Individual timestep multiplier to the ion heat equation applied in the corresponding flux tube from the DTFTS list. Must be positive.

switch.name: SOLVECO
switch.description: Indicates whether the continuity equation for species (is) is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEMO
switch.description: Indicates whether the parallel momentum equation for species (is) is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEMT
switch.description: Indicates whether the total parallel momentum equation is to be solved in region (ireg). For the total parallel momentum equation to be solved in a region, all momentum equations in the range defined by 'b2news_nsmin' and 'b2news_nsmax' must have SOLVEMO true in that region. Subservient to 'no_solve' switch. It is important to note that the 'b2news_no_solve' switch allows for solving or not all momentum equations together as a whole, along with the total equation. Defaults to .true. unless using the time-dependent mode, in which case it is set to .false. and should not be used.

switch.name: SOLVEPO
switch.description: Indicates whether the potential energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEEE
switch.description: Indicates whether the electron energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEEI
switch.description: Indicates whether the ion energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEEN
switch.description: Indicates whether the fluid neutral energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEET
switch.description: Indicates whether the total energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). For the equation to be solved in region (ireg), both SOLVEEE and SOLVEEI must be true in that region. Subservient to 'no_solve' switch. It is important to note that the 'b2news_no_solve' switch allows to solve or not to solve the electron and ion heat equations together with the total energy equation, as a group. Defaults to .true. unless using the time-dependent mode, in which case it is set to .false. and should not be used.

switch.name: SOLVEKT
switch.description: Indicates whether the ExB turbulent kinetic energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEZT
switch.description: Indicates whether the ExB turbulent enstrophy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: TIME_FACTOR_REQUIRED
switch.description: Minimum time scale of evolution allowed for all equations. Only active is 'b2srsm_enable' is set to 1.

switch.name: CORE_DT_SUPPRESSION
switch.description: De-multiplier to the timestep in the core. Only active if less than 1. Should be larger than 0. Applies fully to the innermost core ring of cells (IY .eq. -1). See CORE_DT_FACTOR for further use.

switch.name: CORE_DT_FACTOR
switch.description: Multiplier to the timestep in the core. Only active is less than 1. Should be larger than 0. Multiplies each successive core ring of cells (increasing IY) by CORE_DT_FACTOR, until the local time step multiplier is equal to 1.

switch.name: CORR_CORE_DN
switch.description: Pressure correction speed-up parameter α_a, acting on the density contribution from species a. Will apply in the core region, except for the nrings outer surfaces. See nrings_for_no_speedup_averaging for details. See Pressure_correction_speed-up.pdf in $SOLPSTOP/doc for a full description. Should be roughly equal to corr_core_dt below. Does not apply to neutral species.

switch.name: CORR_CORE_DT
switch.description: Pressure correction speed-up parameter α_T, acting on the temperature contributions. Will apply in the core region, except for the nrings outer surfaces. See nrings_for_no_speedup_averaging for details. See Pressure_correction_speed-up.pdf in $SOLPSTOP/doc for a full description. Should be roughly equal to corr_core_dn above.

switch.name: SNA_CORR
switch.description: Multiplier to the neutral source differential between puffing (plus sputtering plus core boundary flux plus any external sources) and pumping, which is further added to the ionization source for code speed-up. One multiplier must be provided per isonuclear sequence. Such settings might be relevant for e.g. a mixture of main ion and trace impurity. See E. Kaveeva et al., Nucl. Fusion 58 (2018) 126018 for details and keep in mind that in that paper the scheme was described in its original species-unresolved form.

switch.name: TAUMAX
switch.description: Maximum allowed fraction of ionization source for a given isonuclear sequence from its neutral state (coming from Eirene) to be added or subtracted to the real Eirene source in the method of effective sources for code speed-up.

switch.name: DO_SNA_CORR_CORE
switch.description: Parameter which controls exclusion/inclusion of confined region from/in the method of effective sources for code speed-up for each isonuclear sequence. If .true., then the flux through the core boundary of that species is taken into account during the source correction computation, and the source correction is applied on closed flux surfaces (default behavior). If .false., then the particle imbalance is computed as puffing plus sputtering plus any external sources minus pumping without account of the flux through the core boundary, and no source correction is applied on closed surfaces (such a setting might be helpful if the core flux is much bigger than the puffing rate and the core is isolated from the SOL by a strong transport barrier).

switch.name: MIN_NA
switch.description: Species- and region-dependent minimum density. Only used if 'b2mndr_use_min_na_numerics.ne.0'. The default value is the one set by b2mndr_na_min.

switch.name: NUMERICS_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /NUMERICS/ namelist.

switch.name: NUMERICS_TIME_MOD
switch.description: When the B2.5 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(NUMERICS_TIME_MOD), reaches or exceeds NUMERICS_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from NUMERICS_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist as the ELM count (here time/NUMERICS_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if NUMERICS_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: NUMERICS_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: If NUMERICS_TIME_MOD is greater than 0, time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle at which a new NUMERICS namelist is read. Otherwise, B2.5 simulation time, in seconds, when to read the next NUMERICS namelist file from NUMERICS_FILENAME. Only active if NUMERICS_TIME_SWITCH is greater than 0.

switch.name: WRITE_NML_NUM
switch.description: If .true. (default), writes the content of the namelist to stdout after it has been read.

switch.name: ADD_TE_CORR_TO_PO
switch.description: If .true. (default), adds dte(ix,iy)/qe to the potential correction after the internal energy balance equations are solved, where dte(ix,iy) is the electron temperature correction on the time step. Individually set for each region index.

switch.name: ETA_HCE_MULT
switch.description: Used by the user specified set_transport_eta transport model. See code for details.

introduction.description: Allows user to choose between different anomalous transport models. Read if 'b2tqna_transport_namelist' is set to 1. Overwrites data from b2ah.dat and b2mn.dat. Does not apply to fluid neutral species.

switchgroup.name: FLAG*
switchgroup.description: All flags follow: FLAG=0: Use model from b2ah.dat and b2mn.dat. Default. FLAG=1: Constant, value set to PARM. FLAG=2: 1/N model, multiplied by PARM. FLAG=3: Bohm model, multiplied by PARM. FLAG=4: flux-scaled model, multiplied by PARM.

switch.name: FLAG_DNA
switch.description: Transport model flag for density-driven diffusion.

switch.name: FLAG_DPA
switch.description: Transport model flag for pressure-driven dffusion.

switch.name: FLAG_VLA
switch.description: Transport model flag for pinch velocity.

switch.name: FLAG_VSA
switch.description: Transport model flag for viscosity.

switch.name: FLAG_HCI
switch.description: Transport model flag for ion heat diffusivity.

switch.name: FLAG_HCE
switch.description: Transport model flag for electron heat diffusivity.

switch.name: FLAG_SIG
switch.description: Transport model flag for field-driven current radial conductivity.

switch.name: FLAG_ALF
switch.description: Transport model flag for temperature-driven current radial conductivity.

switch.name: PARM_DNA
switch.description: Parameter for the density-driven particle diffusion coefficient for species (is).

switch.name: PARM_DPA
switch.description: Parameter for the pressure-driven particle diffusion coefficient for species (is).

switch.name: PARM_VLA
switch.description: Parameter for the anomalous radial pinch velocity for species (is).

switch.name: PARM_VSA
switch.description: Parameter for the viscosity for species (is).

switch.name: PARM_HCI
switch.description: Parameter for the heat diffusivity coefficient for species (is).

switch.name: PARM_HCE
switch.description: Parameter for the electron heat diffusivity coefficient.

switch.name: PARM_SIG
switch.description: Parameter for the anomalous radial field-driven current conductivity.

switch.name: PARM_ALF
switch.description: Parameter for the anomalous radial temperature-driven current conductivity.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /TRANSPORT/ namelist.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD
switch.description: When the B2.5 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD), reaches or exceeds TRANSPORT_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from TRANSPORT_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist if the ELM count (here time/TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: If TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD is greater than 0, time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle at which a new TRANSPORT namelist is read. Otherwise, B2.5 simulation time, in seconds, when to read the next TRANSPORT namelist file from TRANSPORT_FILENAME. Only active if TRANSPORT_TIME_SWITCH is greater than 0.

switchgroup.name: CFL*
switchgroup.description: For all flux limit multipliers, setting them to zero turns off the corresponding flux limiter. Otherwise, the multipliers must be positive.

switch.name: CFLME
switch.default: value inherited from b2ah.dat (or b2mn.dat if specified in b2cmpt block)
switch.description: Multiplier to the electron heat flux limit.

switch.name: CFLMI
switch.default: value inherited from b2ah.dat (or b2mn.dat if specified in b2cmpt block)
switch.description: Multiplier to the ion heat flux limit.

switch.name: CFLMV
switch.default: value inherited from b2ah.dat (or b2mn.dat if specified in b2cmpt block)
switch.description: Multiplier to the viscous heat flux limit.

switch.name: CFLAL
switch.default: value inherited from b2ah.dat (or b2mn.dat if specified in b2cmpt block)
switch.description: Multiplier to the thermo-electric coefficient flux limit.

switch.name: CFLAB
switch.default: value inherited from b2ah.dat (or b2mn.dat if specified in b2cmpt block)
switch.description: Multiplier to the friction force flux limit.

switch.name: WRITE_NML_TRANSP
switch.description: If .true. (default), writes the content of the namelist to stdout after it has been read.

switchgroup.name: *_CNV
switchgroup.description: The four *_CNV parameters are only activated if 'b2tqna_user_transport' is set to '6'.

switch.name: VOUT_CNV
switch.description: Value of the "blob" convection velocity for species (is) at the outer grid edge (in m/s).

switch.name: PW0_CNV
switch.description: Exponent in radial profile of the "blob" velocity for species (is).

switch.name: PW1_CNV
switch.description: First exponent in poloidal profile of the "blob" velocity for species (is).

switch.name: PW2_CNV
switch.description: Second exponent in a poloidal profile of the "blob" velocity for species (is).

introduction.description: Contains additional data for user-specific diagnostics. Always read if present. This version originally provided by Andrei Kukushkin for ITER cases, however, it may be used (at least partially) for arbitrary machine.

switch.name: RZOMP
switch.description: Coordinates defining a segment. All control volumes intersected by this segment will be part of the outer midplane. rzomp(1,1:2) holds the R-coordinates of the first and second point of the segment. rzomp(2,1:2) holds the Z-coordinates of the first and second point of the segment. Warning! This variable needs always be defined for SOLPS-ITER version 3.2.0 or younger!

switch.name: RZIMP
switch.description: Coordinates defining a segment. All control volumes intersected by this segment will be part of the inner midplane. rzimp(1,1:2) holds the R-coordinates of the first and second point of the segment. rzimp(2,1:2) holds the Z-coordinates of the first and second point of the segment.

switch.name: LHETRGTS
switch.default: Eirene recycling target surfaces defined in the LTNS array
switch.description: List of surface indices (EIRENE notation) which are used for calculation of helium enrichment.

switch.name: LPFRB_I
switch.description: Obsolete. Use PFR_CVS instead. B2.5 x-cell index corresponding to the first edge of the bypass between the inner and outer divertor (Western divertor, edge closest to target). If non-positive, counted backwards from the X-point location in the lower PFR, from the inner upper target in the upper PFR, from the lower outer target in the outer SOL, and from the inner upper target in the inner SOL.

switch.name: LPFRB_O
switch.description: Obsolete. Use PFR_CVS instead. B2.5 x-cell index corresponding to the first edge of the bypass between the inner and outer divertor (Eastern divertor, edge closest to target). If non-positive, counted backwards from the outer lower target in the lower PFR, from the lower outer target in the outer SOL, from the upper outer target in the upper PFR, and from the inner upper target in the inner SOL.

switch.name: LPFRT_I
switch.description: Obsolete. Use PFR_CVS instead. B2.5 x-cell index corresponding to the second edge of the bypass between the inner and outer divertor (Western divertor, edge furthest to target). If non-positive, counted backwards from the X-point location in the PFR, from the outer target in the outer SOL for a SN, from the top targets in the SOL for a DN.

switch.name: LPFRT_O
switch.description: Obsolete. Use PFR_CVS instead. B2.5 x-cell index corresponding to the second edge of the bypass between the inner and outer divertor (Eastern divertor, edge furthest to target). If non-positive, counted backwards from the X-point location in the PFR, from the outer target in the outer SOL for a SN, from the top targets in the SOL for a DN.

switch.name: J_HE_AT
switch.description: Species index of the helium atoms in Eirene. The code attempts to find a match by default.

switch.name: J_NE_AT
switch.description: Species index of the neon atoms in Eirene. The code attempts to find a match by default.

switch.name: J_H_AT
switch.description: Species indices of the hydrogen isotopes in Eirene. If using only one hydrogen species, only the first element needs to be provided. Otherwise the 3 elements correspond to H/D/T. The code attempts to find a match by default.

switch.name: L_H_MOL
switch.description: Number of hydrogen isotope nuclei for molecules in Eirene. If using only one hydrogen species, only the first element needs to be provided. Otherwise the 3 elements correspond to H/D/T. The code attempts to find a match by default.

switch.name: FUSION_POWER
switch.description: Fusion power occuring in core (including neutrons, in Megawatts).

switch.name: SPMP_HE_TO_D
switch.description: Ratio of He to DT pumping speeds (typically, 0.8).

switch.name: LPFRS_PMP
switch.description: Location of the pump. 0 no pump at all (default), 1 - SN of lower DN PFR, 2 - (outer) SOL, 3 - upper DN PFR, 4 - inner SOL for DN

switch.name: NPFRGRP
switch.description: Actual number of surface groups for PFR flows.

switch.name: LPFRGRP
switch.description: List of surface segments for PFR flows.

switch.name: IPFRGRP
switch.description: First positions in this list for each group.

switch.name: JPFRGRP
switch.description: Last positions in this list for each group.

switch.name: GPFRGRP
switch.description: Labels for surface groups for PFR flows.

switch.name: NNTRGRP
switch.description: Actual number of surface groups for neutral data.

switch.name: LNTRGRP
switch.description: List of surface segments for neutral data.

switch.name: INTRGRP
switch.description: First positions in this list for each group.

switch.name: JNTRGRP
switch.description: Last positions in this list for each group.

switch.name: GNTRGRP
switch.description: Labels for the neutral data surface groups.

switch.name: NPFR_CVS
switch.description: Number of control volumes which are written in PFR_CVS.

switch.name: PFR_CVS
switch.description: List of control volumes over which the average neutral pressure for fb_type.eq.20 is calculated.

switch.name: SPMP_NOM
switch.description: Nominal pumping speed.

switch.name: TE_DET_THRESHOLD
switch.description: Electron temperature at the target (in eV) which is used a threshold identifying the transition of the local magnetic flux tube to detachment. The width of the zone with Te less than TE_DET_THRESHOLD (counted from the strike point towards the SOL side) is outputted into user_SPb.trc. This distance monotonically increases with increasing of radiated power fraction or with decreasing of peak target energy loads, therefore it may be used as a measure of the degree of detachment.

switch.name: Q95_ALBLL
switch.description: Safety factor used to estimate the ballooning "alpha" parameter, if not assigned will be estimated as L_conn/(2*pi*R) at the core interface.

switch.name: R0_ALBLL
switch.description: Major radius used to estimate the ballooning "alpha" parameter. If not assigned, will be estimated as 0.5*(Rmin-Rmax) at the core interface.

switch.name: B0_ALBLL
switch.description: Magnetic field used to estimate the ballooning "alpha" parameter. If not assigned, will be estimated as Bomp*R0/Romp at the core interface.

switch.name: TRGSHP
switch.description: Target shaping factor applied to plasma heat loads in peak heat flux estimate calculated by b2mod_usertrc.

switch.name: FILEDATA
switch.description: Switches to turn on/off the tracing files controlled by the ank_tracing switch. The files are defined in b2mod_diag, in order, starting with element 2 of the filedata array: test.trc, residuals.trc, sources.trc, blnn.trc, blne.trc, integral.trc, user.trc, blnm.trc, sepdata.trc. If there is no b2.user.parameters file present, the flags for user.trc are set to .false.. If the tracing files are to be appended but a reading error occurs when opening them, the corresponding filedata element is overwritten to .false..

switch.name: USER_FILENAME
switch.description: Filename where /USER/ namelist is stored.

introduction.description: Read if 'b2optim_namelist' is set to 1. Necessary for sensitivity and optimization runs or for standard B2.5(-EIRENE) runs when MAP cost function is used.

switch.name: NCF
switch.description: Number of cost functions (icf = 1, NCF).

switch.name: CFTYPE
switch.description: Specifies the type of cost function for index icf. Valid options are: 0 - Sum of weighted least squares cost functions. CFSTART, CFEND indicate over which icf indexes the cost functions are to be summed (valid indexes are 1-3 and 7-9). 1 - Difference, on the desired CVs, of calculated electron density and experimental values read from file, normalized with average experimental value (units must be 10^{19}m^{-3}). 2 - Difference, on the desired CVs, of calculated electron temperature and values read from file, normalized with average experimental value (units must be eV). 3 - Difference, on the desired CVs, of calculated ion temperature and values read from file, normalized with average experimental value (units must be eV). 4 - Maximum electron temperature on the desired CVs (output in eV). 5 - Maximum heat flux on the desired FCs (output in W^{2}/m^{2}). 6 - Bayesian MAP cost function. CFSTART, CFEND indicate over which icf indexes the cost functions are to be summed (valid indexes are 1-3 and 7-9). 7 - Difference, on the desired CVs, of calculated electron density radial gradient and experimental values read from file, normalized with average experimental value (units must be 10^{19}m^{-4}). 8 - Difference, on the desired CVs, of calculated electron temperature radial gradient and values read from file, normalized with average experimental value (units must be eV/m). 9 - Difference, on the desired CVs, of calculated ion temperature radial gradient and values read from file, normalized with average experimental value (units must be eV/m). 10 - Difference, on the desired CVs, of calculated ion saturation current (as ne*cs*eV) and values read from file, normalized with average experimental value (units must be kA/m^{2}). 11 - Heat flux peakedness on the desired FCs (output in W^{2}/m^{2}). 12 - Difference, on the desired FCs, of calculated perpendicular or parallel heat flux and values read from file, normalized with average experimental value (units must be in MW^{2}/m^{2}). Use PARALLEL_HF to specify whehter the parallel or perpendicular heat flux is used.

switch.name: PARALLEL_HF
switch.description: Indicates for cost function 12 if the heat flux considered is the one parallel to the magnetic field (TRUE) or perpendicular to the surface (FALSE).

switch.name: CFDEF
switch.description: Indicates how the domain of cost function ifc is specified. Valid options are: 1 - The cost function is defined on the OMP, using the rzomp array. In this case CFSTART,CFEND are not necessary and ignored. 2 - The cost function is defined using the FC labels specified in CFSTART,CFEND. Cost functions requiring CVs or FCs can both be defined in this way. 3 - The cost function is defined using the CV labels specified in CFSTART,CFEND. Only cost functions requiring CVs can be defined in this way. 4 - The cost function is defined using the CF_REG and CF_REGP arrays. Cost functions requiring CVs or FCs can both be defined in this way.

switch.name: CFSTART, CFEND
switch.description: Specify the starting/ending, face (FC) label fcLbl or control volume (CV) label cvLbl where the cost function icf will be evaluated. For CFTYPE = 0 and CFTYPE = 6 they instead identify which cost functions will be summed together.

switch.name: CF_REG
switch.description: Listing for each cost function the corresponding domain CVs or FCs. WARNING! No attempt is made to check if these CVs or FCs are connected, sorted or even available in the current geometry: it is up to the user to make sure the list is correct. mxnCf is a hard coded maximum length defined in b2mod_par_opt.

switch.name: CF_REGP
switch.description: CF_REGP(icf, 1) points, for cost function icf, to the first index in the CF_REG list. CF_REGP(icf, 2) number of items in CF_REG list for cost function icf.

switch.name: CFWEIGHT
switch.description: Multipliers to re-scale each cost function separately.

switch.name: MapToOMP
switch.description: Indicates if cost function icf is to be re-mapped to the OMP, according to CFTYPE. Warning! The mapping is a rigid translation of the data to the OMP and is safe to work only for non-extended grids and for cost functions defined on the same number of elements as the OMP list.

switch.name: NSIGMA
switch.description: Number of standard deviation variables used for Bayesian MAP cost function (normally this should be equal to the number of cost functions summed for CFTYPE = 6, i.e. one for each variable on each domain) (isigma = 1, NSIGMA}).

switch.name: SIGMA
switch.description: Value of the prediction error standard deviation. For absolute STD the units are the same as the experimental data used for the cost function it refers to.

switch.name: SCALE_SIGMA
switch.description: Indicates if SIGMA(isigma) is intended as relative standard deviation (SCALE_SIGMA(isigma)=.TRUE.) or absolute (SCALE_SIGMA(isigma)=.FALSE.).

switch.name: READ_SIGMA
switch.description: (Experimental! Do not use!) Specifies if sigma can be read from the experimental data files cfi.dat.

switch.name: NMEAN
switch.description: Number of predictin error mean variables used for Bayesian MAP cost function.

switch.name: MEAN
switch.description: Value of the predictin error mean.

switch.name: PRIOR_TYPE
switch.description: Defines the type of prior distribution for each parameter for the MAP cost function. Possible values are: 0 - Uniform distribution (i.e. $\pi(\theta)=1.0$). 1 - Uninformative Gaussian prior. 2 - Gamma distribution, defined through mean and standard deviation.

switch.name: PRIOR_PAR
switch.description: Indicates parameters for the prior distributions. For PRIOR_TYPE=0, not used. For PRIOR_TYPE=1 and 2, PRIOR_TYPE(ii,1) indicates the mean and PRIOR_TYPE(ii,2) indicates the standard deviation of the distribution.

switch.name: PRIOR_RANGE
switch.description: Indicates the valid range for the prior distributions, outside of which the program will return an infinite cost function value. PRIOR_RANGE(:,1) sets the lower range and PRIOR_RANGE(:,2) sets the upper range. (p_infty is a parameter currently set to 1e30).

switch.name: SHIFT_CF_DATA
switch.description: For each cost function where experimental data is read, specifies whether or not to shift the separatrix position in the experimental data spatial coordinate as follows: (r - r_{sep}) - SHIFT_VALUE$. Unique integers larger than zero for each cost function means that each has its specific shift value. Using the same integer for two (or more) cost functions makes to code use the same shift value for those cost functions (the first SHIFT_VALUE among the cost function with the same integer is adopted). Possible usage: Thomson scattering data of density and temperature which should be defined in two separate cost functions but come from the same source and thus have the same shift. NSHIFT is then the number of unique integers and is used to define other parameters below other parameters.

switch.name: SHIFT_VALUE
switch.description: Defines the value of the shift in millimeters for each cost function with SHIFT_CF_DATA .neq. 0. Tis is also used as initial guess in case of optimization.

switch.name: SHIFT_PRIOR_TYPE
switch.description: Same as PRIOR_TYPE but defined for each unique cost function shift.

switch.name: SHIFT_PRIOR_PAR
switch.description: Same as PRIOR_PAR but defined for each unique cost function shift.

switch.name: SHIFT_PRIOR_RANGE
switch.description: Same as PRIOR_RANGE but defined for each unique cost function shift.

switch.name: CORR_MODEL
switch.description: Specifies the type of correlation model for the covariance matrix in the MAP cost function.Possible values are: 0 - No correlation, thus simple diagonal covariance matrix. 1 - Correlation model based on exponential decay with a correlation length specified using CORR_LENGTH.

switch.name: CORR_LENGTH
switch.description: Defines the value of the correlation length in millimeters for each MAP-type cost function with CORR_MODEL>0. This is also used as initial guess in case of optimization.

switch.name: CORR_CUTOFF
switch.description: For the exponential decay correlation model, off-diagonal elements with value of the exponential below this threshold are set to zero.

switch.name: CORR_PRIOR_TYPE
switch.description: Same as PRIOR_TYPE but defined for each MAP cost function where sensitivity for CORR_LENGTH is calculated or this parameter optimized (see CORR_OPT). NCORR_OPT is the number of correlation lengths which are optimized, so for which a prior is required.

switch.name: CORR_PRIOR_PAR
switch.description: Same as PRIOR_PAR but defined for each MAP cost function where sensitivity for CORR_LENGTH is calculated or this parameter optimized.

switch.name: CORR_PRIOR_RANGE
switch.description: Same as PRIOR_RANGE but defined for each MAP cost function where sensitivity for CORR_LENGTH is calculated or this parameter optimized.

switch.name: NNVAR
switch.description: Number of sensitivity/optimization parameters (radially varying transport coefficients count as 1).

switch.name: PARTYPE
switch.description: Indicates the type of sensitivity/optimization parameter. Valid options are: -1 - mean, error mean in likelihood function for Bayesian inference (one for each plasma quantity and location used). If present, these must ALWAYS be the last parameters in the vector! 0 - sigma, standard deviation in likelihood function for Bayesian inference (one for each plasma quantity and location used). If present, these must ALWAYS be defined after each physical parameter (PARTYPE>0) and before the mean (PARTYPE=-1). 1 - parm_dna or tdata(:,:,1,:) 2 - parm_dpa or tdata(:,:,2,:) 3 - parm_hci or tdata(:,:,3,:) 4 - parm_hce or tdata(:,:,4,:) 5 - tdata(:,:,5,:) (not yet tested!) 6 - parm_vla or tdata(:,:,6,:) 7 - parm_vsa or tdata(:,:,7,:) 8 - parm_sig or tdata(:,:,8,:) 9 - parm_alf or tdata(:,:,9,:) 10 - enepar 11 - enipar 12 - conpar 13 - mompar 14 - potpar 15 - enkpar 16 - b2recyc 17 - b2tqna_ballooning 18 - b2tqna_ballooning_rescale 19 - keps_cd 20 - keps_heat 21 - keps_heat_i 22 - keps_sig 23 - keps_alf 24 - keps_visc 25 - keps_dkt 26 - keps_dzt 27 - b2sikt_fac_diss 28 - b2sikt_fac_diss_core 29 - b2sikt_fac_sheath 30 - b2sikt_fac_sheath_core 31 - keps_shear 32 - b2tfhi_fsigkt 33 - b2sikt_fac_vis_RS 34 - b2tfhi_fflokt 35 - b2tfhi_fconkt 36 - b2tfhi_fflozt 37 - b2tfhi_fconzt 38 - b2tfhi_fkt_hie 39 - b2tfhe_vis_kt

switch.name: SIGMA_OPT
switch.description: For each sigma, specifies whether its sensitivity is computed or not, and thus whether it is optimized or fixed.

switch.name: MEAN_OPT
switch.description: For each mean, specifies whether its sensitivity is computed or not, and thus whether it is optimized or fixed.

switch.name: SHIFT_OPT
switch.description: For each SHIFT_CF_VALUE, specifies whether its sensitivity is computed or not, and whether is optimized or fixed.

switch.name: CORR_OPT
switch.description: For each MAP cost function where CORR_MODEL>0, specifies whether CORR_LENGTH sensitivity is computed or not, and whether is optimized or fixed.

switch.name: SPATIAL_DEP
switch.description: For each sensitivity/optimization variables, specifies if radially varying coefficients are optimized. Meaningful only for partype = 1 to 9.

switch.name: SPATIAL_POINTS
switch.description: Number of spatial points for sensitivity/optimization parameter ipar (should be the same as ndata in b2.transport.inputfile). The actual number of optimized variables (NPAR_OPT) is then equal to the total number of spatial points + any other additional parameter.

switch.name: PARIS
switch.description: Specifies the species of the sensitivity/optimization parameter. Only meaningful for partype = [1-3,5,7,12,13,16].

switch.name: PARIB
switch.description: Specifies the boundary/strata of the sensitivity/optimization parameter. Only meaningful for partype = [10-16].

switch.name: PAR_RESCALE
switch.description: Coefficient to rescale optimization parameters (and their gradient). Note dimension is NPAR_OPT, so need to specify it also for each point in case of spatially varying coefficients.

switch.name: X0
switch.description: Initial guess for each optimization parameter. Must be defined (p_infty is a parameter currently set to 1e30).

switch.name: XL
switch.description: Lower bound for each optimization parameter.

switch.name: XU
switch.description: Upper bound for each optimization parameter.

switch.name: SHIFT_L
switch.description: Lower bound for SHIFT_CF_DATA parameters.

switch.name: SHIFT_U
switch.description: Upper bound for SHIFT_CF_DATA parameters.

switch.name: CORR_L
switch.description: Lower bound for CORR_LENGTH parameters. Must be specified for each cost function!

switch.name: CORR_U
switch.description: Upper bound for CORR_LENGTH parameters. Must be specified for each cost function!

switch.name: CORR_RESCALE
switch.description: Same as PAR_RESCALE but defined for CORR_LENGTH} parameters. Must be specified for each cost function!

switch.name: MAXITER
switch.description: Maximum number of optimization iterations. Can overridden with PETCs/Tao by using command line options for the optimization.

switch.name: CPU_OPT
switch.description: Maximum amount of CPU time in seconds after which the optimization is stopped.

switch.name: TOL_OPT
switch.description: Tolerance below which optimization is stopped. For PETCs/Tao it is used for the absolute gradient norm, the relative gradient norm, and the gradient reduction stopping criteria. Can be overridden with PETCs/Tao by using command line options for the optimization.

switch.name: HESSIAN_APPROXIMATION
switch.description: Type of Hessian approximation employed. Depends on optimization library. Possible values are currently: 'limited-memory' and 'exact'. When using the 'exact' option the steepest descent algorithm is used.

switch.name: LIMITED_MEMORY_UPDATE_TYPE
switch.description: Specifies which kind of Hessian approximation is employed if HESSIAN_APPROXIMATION is set to 'limited-memory'. Can overridden with PETCs/Tao by using command line options for the optimization.

introduction.description: Read if file is present. Used to regulate the temporal evolution of sputtering and restarts. Not intended for human use.

switch.name: NX_SP
switch.description: Array dimension NX used in this namelist.

switch.name: NY_SP
switch.description: Array dimension NY used in this namelist.

switch.name: NS_SP
switch.description: Array dimension NS used in this namelist.

switch.name: SPUTTER_YIELD
switch.description: Contains the chemical sputtering yield of species (is) at position (ix,iy) in element (:,:,:,1) and the physical sputtering yield in element (:,:,:,2).

switch.name: SPUTTER_YIELD2
switch.description: Contains the energy chemical sputtering yield of species (is) at position (ix,iy) in element (:,:,:,1) and the energy physical sputtering yield in element(:,:,:,2).

introduction.description: Read if 'b2mndr_atomic_physics_rescale' is set to 1. Contains rescaling multipliers for atomic physics rates.

switch.name: RESCALE_SA
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtsa: ionisation rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_RA
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtra: recombination rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_QA
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtqa: electron cooling rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_CX
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtcx: charge exchange rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_RD
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtrd: line radiation rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_BR
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtbr: bremsstrahlung radiation rates of species (is).
